In Backend/StoredProcedureToLinq/sp_HSCodeReport.StoredProcedure.cs add `CancellationToken cancellationToken` (add `using System.Threading;`) to `ExecuteAggregateStoredProcedureAsync(...)` and change line 51 to `.ToListAsync(cancellationToken);`

In Backend/StoredProcedureToLinq/sp_HSCodeReport.cs (add `using System.Threading;`) thread the token through: add `CancellationToken cancellationToken = default` to `CreateAggregateResultAsync(...)`, forward it to `await ExecuteAggregateStoredProcedureAsync(db, request, pageIndex, pageSize, cancellationToken)` and to `await ApiResult<ReportAggregateResult>.CreateFastPageAsync(query, ..., pagingRequest.IncludeTotalCount, cancellationToken)`. Also add the optional token to `GetAggregateRowsAsync(...)` -> `await AggregateQuery(db, request).ToListAsync(cancellationToken)` so the streaming WriteRowsAsync can pass its existing token (optional but consistent).

Update every HSCode aggregate controller's Post action to accept and forward the token. There are 8: ImportLicenceByHSCodeReportController.cs, ExportLicenceByHSCodeReportController.cs, ImportPermitByHSCodeReportController.cs, ExportPermitByHSCodeReportController.cs, BorderImportLicenceByHSCodeReportController.cs, BorderExportLicenceByHSCodeReportController.cs, BorderImportPermitByHSCodeReportController.cs, BorderExportPermitByHSCodeReportController.cs. In each, change `Post([FromBody] XxxRequest? request)` to `Post([FromBody] XxxRequest? request, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` and `await sp_HSCodeReport.CreateAggregateResultAsync(_context, procedureRequest!, request!)` to `await sp_HSCodeReport.CreateAggregateResultAsync(_context, procedureRequest!, request!, cancellationToken)`. (`using System.Threading;` is already imported in these files.)

Fix the ImportLicenceBySectionReportController.cs path (cited as the worst offender). Add `CancellationToken cancellationToken` to `Post([FromBody] ImportLicenceBySectionReportRequest? request, CancellationToken cancellationToken)`. Add the token to `BuildRowsAsync(ImportLicenceBySectionReportRequest request, CancellationToken cancellationToken)` and forward it from the GetOrCreateAsync delegate: `return await BuildRowsAsync(request, entry.Token == default ? cancellationToken : cancellationToken);` (simplest: just pass `cancellationToken`). Inside BuildRowsAsync change the fully-synchronous `.ToList()` at line 125 to `.ToListAsync(cancellationToken)` (make the LINQ assignment `await`), and add the token to the two existing `.ToListAsync()` calls at lines 126-127: `await _context.Currencies.AsNoTracking().Where(x => x.Code != null).ToListAsync(cancellationToken)` and `await _context.ExportImportSections.Where(x => x.Type == "Import Licence").AsNoTracking().ToListAsync(cancellationToken)`.
Build to catch any missed call sites: `dotnet build` from the Backend project directory. Fix any optional-parameter ambiguity or missing-token compile errors it surfaces.
verification: dotnet build the Backend project — no missed call sites and no optional-parameter overload errors.

Manual abort test: POST a slow report (e.g. /api/AccountSummaryReport or /api/ImportLicenceBySectionReport with a wide FromDate/ToDate range) and abort the client mid-flight (curl --max-time 2, or cancel the browser request). Watch SQL Server sys.dm_exec_requests (or Activity Monitor) — the report query should disappear within ~1s of the abort instead of running to the 120/180s command timeout.

Confirm the EF/SqlClient command is actually cancelled: the cancelled request should surface as an OperationCanceledException in logs (ASP.NET Core treats RequestAborted-driven cancellation as a 499/aborted response, not a 500).

Regression: a normal (non-aborted) report request still returns the same paged ApiResult JSON with correct Data/TotalCount; the Excel queue path (POST .../Excel then background WriteRowsAsync) still produces the workbook unchanged.
pitfalls: Do the APIResult.cs and ReportQueryService.cs signature changes FIRST; the controllers and sp_* helpers call into them, so leading edits will not compile until the shared helpers accept the token.; Add `CancellationToken cancellationToken = default` (optional) on shared/library methods (ApiResult, ReportQueryService, sp_HSCodeReport.CreateAggregateResultAsync/GetAggregateRowsAsync, sp_AccountSummaryReport.ExecuteAsync) so the many other callers that do not yet pass a token keep compiling. Make it non-optional ONLY on controller Post actions (model binding always supplies HttpContext.RequestAborted).; ExecuteQueryable in sp_AccountSummaryReport returns IQueryable and must stay token-free — only its ToListAsync terminal (in ExecuteAsync) takes the token. Same for sp_HSCodeReport.Query/AggregateQuery.; Do NOT modify the IStreamingExcelReport.WriteRowsAsync signature or the Excel ChunkAsync path; it already receives and propagates a token from the background worker. This finding is only about the synchronous JSON Post actions.; In ImportLicenceBySectionReportController the rows are wrapped in IMemoryCache.GetOrCreateAsync; passing the token into BuildRowsAsync means an aborted first request can cancel the cache population so a concurrent waiter may get no cached value — acceptable, but do not store a partially-built/cancelled list in the cache (GetOrCreateAsync already discards on exception, so an OperationCanceledException leaves the entry unpopulated, which is correct).; Rollback is a clean revert: every change is additive (new optional params + token forwarding) with no schema, migration, or stored-procedure change.

approach: Turn on ASP.NET Core's built-in structured logging (JSON console formatter), add the framework HttpLogging middleware plus a tiny correlation-id middleware that reads/echoes X-Correlation-Id and pushes it into the log scope, then inject ILogger into report controllers (starting with AccountSummaryReportController) to log report key, user, and duration around execution. No third-party package is required since Microsoft.Extensions.Logging is already referenced.
steps: In Backend/Program.cs, immediately after `var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);` (line 21), configure structured JSON logging: `builder.Logging.ClearProviders(); builder.Logging.AddJsonConsole(o => { o.IncludeScopes = true; o.UseUtcTimestamp = true; });` and in Development also add `builder.Logging.AddDebug();`. IncludeScopes=true is what makes the correlation id (added via a log scope below) appear on every log line.

In Backend/Program.cs service-registration block (around line 95, after `builder.Services.AddExcelExportQueue(...)`), register HTTP request logging: `builder.Services.AddHttpLogging(o => { o.LoggingFields = HttpLoggingFields.RequestPath | HttpLoggingFields.RequestMethod | HttpLoggingFields.ResponseStatusCode | HttpLoggingFields.Duration; });` Add `using Microsoft.AspNetCore.HttpLogging;` to the using block at the top (lines 1-15).

Create a correlation-id middleware file Backend/Middleware/CorrelationIdMiddleware.cs (new folder). It reads the incoming `X-Correlation-Id` header (or generates a Guid if absent), stores it in HttpContext.Items, echoes it on the response via context.Response.OnStarting, and wraps the rest of the pipeline in `logger.BeginScope` so the id is attached to every log entry for that request. See the patch for the full class plus the `UseCorrelationId` extension method.

In Backend/Program.cs request-pipeline section, add the middleware EARLY so the correlation scope wraps everything — place `app.UseCorrelationId();` then `app.UseHttpLogging();` right after `app.UseRouting();` (line 160) and before `app.UseAuthentication();` (line 166). Order matters: correlation scope must be outermost so HttpLogging and controller logs inherit it. Add `using API.Middleware;` to the usings.

In Backend/appsettings.json, expand the Logging section so framework noise stays low while the app namespace logs at Information. Replace lines 2-7 with the LogLevel block in the patch (add `"Microsoft.AspNetCore.HttpLogging.HttpLoggingMiddleware": "Information"`, `"API": "Information"`, `"Backend": "Information"`). Keep `"Microsoft.AspNetCore": "Warning"` for the rest. Do NOT add anything that logs request/response bodies (sensitive trade data).

Add an ILogger to the representative controller as the rollout template: in Backend/Controllers/Report/AccountSummaryReportController.cs add field `private readonly ILogger<AccountSummaryReportController> _logger;`, add the parameter to the constructor at lines 35-39 (`ILogger<AccountSummaryReportController> logger`) and assign `_logger = logger;`. Add `using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging;` to the usings (lines 1-15).

In AccountSummaryReportController.Post (lines 42-77), wrap report execution with timing and logging. Before the `try` capture `var sw = System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch.StartNew();` and `var user = User.FindFirst(ClaimTypes.Name)?.Value;` (ClaimTypes is already imported, line 4). On success log `_logger.LogInformation("Report {ReportKey} executed by {User} returned {RowCount} rows in {ElapsedMs}ms", ReportKey, user, data.Count, sw.ElapsedMilliseconds);` and in the SqlException-fallback catch (line 70) log `_logger.LogWarning(ex, "Report {ReportKey} pagination proc missing, falling back to LINQ query for {User}", ReportKey, user);`. See patch.

Repeat steps 6-7 across the other 157 report controllers in Backend/Controllers/Report and the JWT/lookup controllers as a follow-up rollout. They share the same ctor+ReportKey shape, so the change is mechanical. Prioritize the controllers tied to the recurring wrong-total complaints first.
verification: Build: `dotnet build "Backend/API.csproj"` succeeds.

Run the API and call a report, e.g. `curl -i -X POST http://localhost:<port>/api/AccountSummaryReport -H 'Authorization: Bearer <jwt>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}'` — confirm the response includes an `X-Correlation-Id` header.
Send your own id: `curl -i ... -H 'X-Correlation-Id: test-123'` and confirm the same `test-123` is echoed back in the response header.

Check the console: each request emits JSON log lines (HttpLogging request/response + the controller's 'Report AccountSummaryReport executed by ... returned N rows in Mms' line), and every line carries the same CorrelationId in its Scopes.
Negative check: confirm no request/response BODY appears in logs (only path, method, status, duration) so sensitive trade data is not logged.
pitfalls: Middleware ordering: UseCorrelationId must come before UseHttpLogging and before UseAuthentication so the correlation scope wraps every downstream log; placing it after MapControllers would miss controller logs. IncludeScopes=true on the JSON console is required or the correlation id won't render. Do NOT enable HttpLoggingFields.RequestBody/ResponseBody — this system handles sensitive trade data and bodies would leak into logs. ClearProviders() removes the default console writer, so AddJsonConsole must stay or you lose all output. Adding the ILogger ctor param is a non-breaking DI change (controllers are container-resolved), but update any test that instantiates a controller manually. Rolling out to all 158 controllers is mechanical but large; do it incrementally with AccountSummaryReportController as the reference. Note: appsettings.json also ships plaintext sa DB credentials and a hardcoded JWT key (lines 10, 26-27) — out of scope for this finding, track separately.
estimatedTime: Half a day for Program.cs + middleware + appsettings + reference controller; ~1-2 additional days to roll ILogger across the remaining controllers.

approach: Add Xunit.SkippableFact and gate the three live-SQL-Server test classes (ReportEndpointSmokeTests DB theories, ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests, TempSectionValidation) behind a one-time CanConnect probe via Skip.IfNot so they skip (not fail) when no server is present; keep the existing server-free tier runnable, and wire a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the server-free tier always and the SQL tier against an mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server service container.
steps: Add the package to Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj inside the existing <ItemGroup> with PackageReferences: <PackageReference Include="Xunit.SkippableFact" Version="1.5.23" />. (1.5.x targets xunit 2.x, matching the project's xunit 2.9.3.)

Create a shared probe helper Backend.Tests/SqlServerAvailability.cs that, given a Func<TradeNetDbContext>, caches a single CanConnectAsync result per connection target so the probe runs once instead of per-theory-case. Wrap CanConnectAsync in try/catch and treat any exception (e.g. localdb missing on Linux) as 'not available' rather than letting it throw. Expose a static IsAvailable(Func<TradeNetDbContext> factory, string key) returning bool.

In Backend.Tests/ReportTestHelper.cs, replace the hard-coded localdb connection string in CreateSqlServerDbContext (line 122: "Server=(localdb)\\mssqllocaldb;...") with an env-var override: read TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_SQLSERVER (or reuse TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_CONNECTION_STRING) and only fall back to the localdb string when it is unset. Substitute the database name into the resolved connection string's Database/Initial Catalog. This lets CI point the endpoint fixture at the mssql service container without code changes.

In Backend.Tests/ReportEndpointSmokeTests.cs, change the two DB-touching theories (Report_post_endpoint_returns_empty_page_for_empty_authenticated_fixture line 36, and Report_excel_endpoint_returns_xlsx_or_queues_job_for_empty_authenticated_fixture line 49) from [Theory] to [SkippableTheory] and add as their first line: Skip.IfNot(SqlServerAvailability.IsAvailable(sqlServerFixture.CreateDbContext, "endpoint-fixture"), "SQL Server fixture unavailable."); Leave the reflection-only Report_controller_is_authorized_and_exposes_post_and_excel_routes theory (line 13) as a plain [Theory] — it needs no DB.

Guard the fixture itself in Backend.Tests/ReportSqlServerFixture.cs so InitializeAsync (lines 13-19) does not throw on a server-less machine: probe CanConnectAsync before EnsureDeletedAsync/EnsureCreatedAsync, set a field like IsAvailable=false and return early when it cannot connect (do not call EnsureCreatedAsync). The SkippableTheory guard in step 4 then short-circuits cleanly. Make DisposeAsync (lines 21-25) likewise skip EnsureDeletedAsync when not available.

In Backend.Tests/ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests.cs, convert TradeNetDBTest_database_is_available (line 9), Report_post_endpoint_generates_seeded_database_page (line 21) and Representative_report_modules_match_seeded_database_row_counts (line 38) to [SkippableFact]/[SkippableTheory]. For the connectivity test, replace the failing Assert.True(await db.Database.CanConnectAsync(), ...) with Skip.IfNot(await db.Database.CanConnectAsync(), "TradeNetDBTest unavailable.") so absence skips rather than fails the class. For the two theories, add Skip.IfNot(SqlServerAvailability.IsAvailable(ReportTestHelper.CreateTradeNetDbTestDbContext, "tradenetdbtest"), "TradeNetDBTest unavailable.") as the first statement.

In Backend.Tests/TempSectionValidation.cs, change Section_runs_and_is_fast (line 33) to [SkippableTheory] and replace the throw in NewDb() (lines 17-22) path with a skip: read TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_CONNECTION_STRING and call Skip.If(string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(connectionString), "TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_CONNECTION_STRING not set.") then Skip.IfNot(SqlServerAvailability.IsAvailable(...)) at the start of the test, rather than NewDb throwing InvalidOperationException (which currently fails the test instead of skipping).

Add a CI workflow .github/workflows/backend-tests.yml (the repo has no .github/workflows today). Job 'server-free' runs on ubuntu-latest with dotnet 8, executing: dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName!~ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests&FullyQualifiedName!~TempSectionValidation". Job 'sql' on ubuntu-latest declares a services: block with image mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest, env ACCEPT_EULA=Y and SA_PASSWORD, port 1433, and a health check; it sets TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_SQLSERVER and TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_CONNECTION_STRING to that container and runs the full dotnet test. With the skip guards, the seeded/TempSection theories that need pre-seeded data will Skip (the service container starts empty) instead of failing, while the endpoint smoke theories run green against the fresh DB.
verification: dotnet build Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj succeeds with the new Xunit.SkippableFact reference.

On a machine WITHOUT SQL Server (e.g. clean Linux): dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj completes with 0 failures and reports the ReportEndpointSmokeTests DB theories, ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests, and TempSectionValidation as Skipped (not Failed). Confirm with: dotnet test ... --logger "console;verbosity=detailed" and look for 'Skipped' lines mentioning the skip reasons.

Confirm the server-free tier still actually runs (not all-skipped): ReportControllerBranchDefaultsTests, ReportEndpointPayloadFixtureTests, ReportLookupCacheRegressionTests, ReportQueryTranslationTests, StreamingExcelWriterTests, and the reflection-only ReportEndpointSmokeTests.Report_controller_is_authorized_and_exposes_post_and_excel_routes all show Passed.

With a SQL Server present and TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_SQLSERVER set: re-run dotnet test and confirm the ReportEndpointSmokeTests Post/Excel theories now execute and Pass (proving the env-var override and Skip.IfNot pass-through work).
Push the branch and confirm the new GitHub Actions workflow runs both jobs: 'server-free' green with no SQL Server, and 'sql' green against the mssql service container.
pitfalls: Do NOT touch ReportQueryTranslationTests.cs even though it calls CreateSqlServerDbContext — it only calls ToQueryString() (LINQ-to-SQL translation), which never opens a connection, so it is already CI-safe. Wrapping it in Skip guards would wrongly skip real coverage. (Changing the connection string in CreateSqlServerDbContext is still safe for it, since translation does not validate the server.); [SkippableFact]/[SkippableTheory] live in the Xunit namespace via the package; Skip is Xunit.Skip. The existing 'global using Xunit;' in the csproj covers SkippableFact/SkippableTheory, but verify Skip resolves — add 'using Xunit;' if a file lacks it.; Run the CanConnect probe once (cache by key) — calling CanConnectAsync per theory case multiplies network timeouts across the ~134 controller cases and makes a no-DB run slow. The SqlServerAvailability cache prevents this.; The mssql service container starts empty, so seeded-data assertions in Representative_report_modules_match_seeded_database_row_counts will not match unless you seed it. The Skip guards keep CI green by skipping data-dependent theories; if you want them to actually run in CI you must add a seed step (restore TradeNetDBTest .bak via sqlcmd) before dotnet test.; Microsoft.Data.SqlClient is already transitively available via EF Core's SqlServer provider; if SqlConnectionStringBuilder does not resolve, add an explicit <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Data.SqlClient" /> or build the connection string by string concatenation instead.; Rollback is low-risk and code-local: revert the attribute/Skip edits and remove the package reference and workflow file.

approach: Add a pure xUnit unit-test class to the existing Backend.Tests project that drives the static ReportAggregationService directly with hand-built AggregateSourceRow lists (no DB), asserting exact values for distinct/case-insensitive licence counting, currency splitting, page-wide column totals, Daily USD rollup, paging clamps, and Currency tie-break ordering.
steps: Create a new file Backend.Tests/ReportAggregationServiceTests.cs in the existing test project (Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj). No csproj changes are needed: it already references ..\Backend\API.csproj and has xunit 2.9.3 with the global Using for Xunit enabled.

Add usings: `using API.Model;` (for ReportQueryRequest / ApiResult) and `using API.Service.Reports;` (for ReportAggregationService, AggregateSourceRow, ReportAggregateResult, ReportAggregateDimension). Use file-scoped `namespace Backend.Tests;` and mark it `public sealed class ReportAggregationServiceTests` to match the style of the other test files (e.g. ReportQueryTranslationTests.cs).

Test (a) distinct + case-insensitive licence count: build a single Section group with LicenceNo values "L-1", "l-1" (case-insensitive dup), "L-2", and one "" (blank). Call ReportAggregationService.Aggregate(rows, ReportAggregateDimension.Section, includeSakhan:false) and assert the single result has NoOfLicences == 2 (the line 112-116 chain: Where(!IsNullOrEmpty) then Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)).

Test (b) currency split: build two rows with the same SectionName "A" but Currency "USD" and "MMK". Assert Aggregate(..., Section, false) returns 2 results (BuildKey at line 277-287 always includes Currency in the key) and TotalValue is summed per currency group, not merged.

Test (c) page-wide column totals: build 3 groups, call ReportAggregationService.CreatePagedResult(rows, Section, includeSakhan:false, new ReportQueryRequest{ PageIndex=0, PageSize=1 }, includeColumnTotals:true). Assert result.Data.Count == 1 (paged) but result.ColumnTotals!["noOfLicences"] and result.ColumnTotals!["totalValue"] equal the sum across ALL groups (BuildColumnTotals at line 227-243 sums the full aggregated list, not pageRows). ColumnTotals is IReadOnlyDictionary<string,decimal>?, so Assert.NotNull then index by key.

Test (d) Daily USD rollup: Aggregate() always sets TotalUSDValue = null (line 118), so test the rollup via ReportAggregationService.CreatePagedResultFromGroups(grouped, ReportAggregateDimension.Daily, false, request, includeColumnTotals:true) with ReportAggregateResult objects whose TotalUSDValue is pre-populated. Assert ColumnTotals contains key "totalUSDValue" equal to decimal.Round(sum of TotalUSDValue, 4) (line 237-240). Add a companion assertion that for a non-Daily dimension the "totalUSDValue" key is absent.

Test (e) paging clamps via [Theory]/[InlineData]: PageSize 0 -> result.PageSize == 10 (DefaultPageSize, lines 137-139); PageSize 5000 -> result.PageSize == 1000 (MaxPageSize); PageIndex -3 -> result.PageIndex == 0 (Math.Max(0, ...) line 136). Assert against the final ApiResult.PageSize/PageIndex.

Test (f) Currency tie-break ordering: build two rows in the same primary-order bucket (same SectionName "A") with Currency "USD" and "MMK"; assert returned order has "MMK" before "USD" (Order() final .ThenBy(row => row.Currency, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase) at line 313).

Compute every expected number (counts, sums, rounded USD totals) by hand from the legacy RDLC semantics and hard-code them as the Assert.Equal expected value -- do not recompute them with LINQ inside the test, otherwise the test would mirror any production bug and stay green.
verification: Run only the new class: `dotnet test "/Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj" --filter FullyQualifiedName~ReportAggregationServiceTests` -- all facts and theory cases should pass.

Mutation/sanity check that the suite actually guards the engine: temporarily change ReportAggregationService.cs line 115 from `.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)` to `.Distinct()` and re-run -- the case-insensitive count test must FAIL (it would report 3 instead of 2). Revert.

Second mutation check: change BuildColumnTotals (line 233-234) to sum the page rows instead of the full aggregated/groups list and confirm the page-wide totals test FAILS, proving it catches the documented missing-TOTAL / wrong-total complaint class. Revert.
Confirm the whole backend suite still builds and passes: `dotnet test "/Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj"`.
pitfalls: PageSize is clamped twice: ReportAggregationService caps it at MaxPageSize 1000 (lines 137-139), then ApiResult.CreatePageFromRows -> NormalizePageSize re-applies the `<=0 -> 10` rule (lines 204-207). Assert against the final ApiResult.PageSize, and note the 1000 cap is ONLY enforced inside the service, so test it via CreatePagedResult (not by constructing ApiResult directly). Aggregate() itself returns TotalUSDValue == null (line 118) for EVERY dimension including Daily -- the USD value is populated later by ReportUsdConversionService -- so the Daily USD rollup must be tested through CreatePagedResultFromGroups with pre-populated TotalUSDValue (as in the patch), not through Aggregate(IEnumerable<AggregateSourceRow>). Use decimal literals (10m, 12.0001m), never doubles, to avoid binary float rounding noise in equality asserts. Do not recompute expected sums/counts with LINQ inside the assertions -- hard-code hand-verified numbers, otherwise a regression in the service would be mirrored by the test and stay green.

approach: Add a table-driven xUnit test class in the existing Backend.Tests project that exercises the FX logic end-to-end through the public ReportUsdConversionService.FillDailyUsdValuesAsync, seeding Currencies/ExchangeRates into an InMemory TradeNetDbContext (via the existing ReportTestHelper.CreateInMemoryDbContext). This covers every branch of the private ConvertToUsd (USD pass-through, normal currency, KRW/JPY per-100, missing item rate, missing USD rate, usdRate==0, lowest-Id dedup) without touching production code.
steps: Create a new test file Backend.Tests/ReportUsdConversionServiceTests.cs in the existing Backend.Tests project (it already references Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory 9.0.6 and ..\Backend\API.csproj, so no csproj or InternalsVisibleTo change is needed).

Use namespace Backend.Tests and add usings: API.DBContext; API.Model.TradeNet (for Currency and ExchangeRate); API.Service.Reports (for ReportUsdConversionService and ReportAggregateResult). Xunit is already globally imported via the <Using Include="Xunit" /> in Backend.Tests.csproj.

Test through the public static method ReportUsdConversionService.FillDailyUsdValuesAsync(db, groups) rather than the private ConvertToUsd. Build each ReportAggregateResult with Date set to a 'yyyy-MM-dd' string (ParseGroupDate only accepts that exact format), Currency set to the code under test, and TotalValue set to the input amount; then assert group.TotalUSDValue after the call.

Add a private helper Seed(TradeNetDbContext db, ...) that inserts Currency rows (Id, Code) and ExchangeRate rows (Id, CurrencyId, Rate, Date) and calls db.SaveChanges(). Use a fresh database name per test (e.g. Guid.NewGuid().ToString()) passed to ReportTestHelper.CreateInMemoryDbContext so InMemory stores do not bleed between cases. IMPORTANT: seed ExchangeRate.Date as the same calendar day the group uses (the query filters rate.Date >= minDate && < dates.Max().AddDays(1) and the dictionary key uses rate.Date.Date), and seed Currency.Code with the EXACT same casing as the group's Currency, because the InMemory provider evaluates currencyCodes.Contains(currency.Code) case-sensitively (production relies on SQL's case-insensitive collation).

Add the following [Theory]/[Fact] cases asserting exact decimal.Round(...,4) results: (a) USD pass-through: Currency="USD", TotalValue=1234.56789m -> expect decimal.Round(1234.56789m,4)=1234.5679m, and it must return that even with NO USD ExchangeRate row seeded (line 125-128 short-circuits before any rate lookup). (b) Normal currency EUR: seed USD rate=2100 MMK, EUR rate=2300 MMK, amount=100 -> expect Round(100*(2300/2100),4)=109.5238m. (c) Per-hundred KRW: seed USD rate=2100, KRW rate=160 (per 100 units), amount=1000 -> factor=(160/2100)/100, expect Round(1000*((160m/2100m)/100m),4)=0.7619m; add an identical JPY case to prove both members of PerHundredCurrencies. (d) Missing item rate -> falls back to 1: seed only the USD rate (no GBP row), Currency="GBP", USD rate=2100, amount=2100 -> currencyRate=1m so expect Round(2100*(1/2100),4)=1.0000m. (e) Missing USD rate -> usdRate falls back to 1: seed only a CAD rate=2500, NO USD row, amount=2 -> expect Round(2*(2500/1),4)=5000.0000m. (f) usdRate==0 -> null: seed USD rate=0m and a EUR rate, amount=100 -> assert group.TotalUSDValue is null (line 134-137). (g) Lowest-Id dedup: seed TWO USD rows for the same date (Id=1 Rate=2000, Id=2 Rate=9999) and a EUR row, amount=100 -> expect the Id=1 (2000) rate is used, NOT 2100/9999 (lines 94-102 keep the first by rate.Id ordering); assert the EUR result computed against 2000.

Add a no-op / guard case: a group whose Date is null or not 'yyyy-MM-dd' (e.g. "2026/06/06") leaves TotalUSDValue null (ConvertToUsd line 120-123 returns null when date is null), and an empty groups list returns without throwing.
Run the suite with: dotnet test "Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj" --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ReportUsdConversionServiceTests" and confirm all cases pass.

(Optional, only if you also want to unit-test ConvertToUsd in isolation without DB seeding) Add [assembly: System.Runtime.CompilerServices.InternalsVisibleTo("Backend.Tests")] to a new Backend/Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs and change ConvertToUsd's modifier from 'private static' to 'internal static' in Backend/Service/Reports/ReportUsdConversionService.cs. This is NOT required for the coverage above and adds production surface area, so prefer the public-method approach unless direct dictionary-input testing is desired.
verification: Run: dotnet test "Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj" --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ReportUsdConversionServiceTests" — all cases (USD passthrough, EUR normal, KRW & JPY per-100, missing item rate, missing USD rate, zero USD rate, duplicate-Id dedup, unparseable date / empty list) pass.
Confirm the previously-failing grep now returns hits: grep -rn "FillDailyUsdValuesAsync\|ReportUsdConversionService\|TotalUSDValue" Backend.Tests should list the new test file.
Run the full suite (dotnet test from the repo root or against tradenet-admin-report.sln) to ensure no regressions and that the new tests compile against the existing InMemory + API.csproj references.
Sanity check a branch by temporarily breaking production code: change 'factor /= 100m;' to 'factor /= 10m;' in ReportUsdConversionService.cs and confirm the KRW and JPY tests fail; revert.
pitfalls: "InMemory provider evaluates currencyCodes.Contains(currency.Code) with case-sensitive .NET string comparison, unlike SQL Server's case-insensitive collation used in production. Seed Currency.Code with the exact casing the group's Currency uses (the tests use uppercase on both sides), or the rate join silently returns no rows and you would be testing the fallback path by accident. The production code is fine; this is purely a test-fidelity concern. Seed each ExchangeRate.Date on the same calendar day the group's Date string parses to — the query window is [minDate, dates.Max().AddDays(1)) and the dedup dictionary keys on rate.Date.Date, so an off-by-one date drops the row. Use a unique InMemory database name per test (Guid) because InMemory stores are keyed by name and persist for the process; reusing a name leaks seeded rows between cases. The optional InternalsVisibleTo step touches production code (Backend/Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs + changing ConvertToUsd to internal) — skip it unless you specifically need to test ConvertToUsd with raw dictionary inputs; the public-method tests already cover every branch. Assert against exact decimal.Round(value,4) results (decimals, not doubles) so rounding-mode regressions are caught."

approach: Add a dedicated xUnit test class for ExcelExportController that wires it up with EF InMemory ApplicationDbContext and a hand-rolled fake IExcelExportFileStore, pinning Download's NotFound/Conflict/410/File branches, Delete's NotFound/NoContent + row removal, and one explicit test that documents the shared-visibility / delete-any-job authorization model so any future regression in object-level auth fails the build.
steps: Create a new test file Backend.Tests/ExcelExportControllerTests.cs (sibling to StreamingExcelWriterTests.cs). The existing ReportTestHelper deliberately scopes to namespace 'Backend.Controllers.Report' (ReportTestHelper.cs:27) and builds controllers on TradeNetDbContext, but ExcelExportController is in namespace Backend.Controllers and depends on ApplicationDbContext + IExcelExportFileStore — so write a self-contained test class rather than extending that harness.

Add usings: `using API.DBContext;` (ApplicationDbContext), `using API.Model.ExcelExport;` (ExcelExportJob, ExcelExportJobStatus), `using API.Service.ExcelExport;` (IExcelExportFileStore, StreamingExcelWriter for ContentType), `using Backend.Controllers;` (ExcelExportController), `using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http;` (StatusCodes), `using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;` (NotFoundResult, ConflictObjectResult, ObjectResult, FileStreamResult, NoContentResult), and `using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;`. (ImplicitUsings is enabled and `Xunit` is globally imported via the csproj, so System/Linq/Threading.Tasks and Xunit do not need explicit usings.)

Add a private helper `static ApplicationDbContext NewDb(string name)` that builds `new DbContextOptionsBuilder<ApplicationDbContext>().UseInMemoryDatabase(name).Options` and returns `new ApplicationDbContext(options)`. Give each test a unique database name (e.g. the test method name) so the in-memory stores don't bleed across tests.

Add a `FakeFileStore : IExcelExportFileStore` nested class implementing all six interface members (BuildRelativePath, OpenWrite, OpenRead, Exists, GetSize, Delete). Make behavior controllable: an `ExistingPaths` HashSet<string> backing `Exists`, an `OpenRead` that returns `new MemoryStream(...)`, and a `DeletedPaths` List<string> recording Delete calls so the Delete test can assert the file-store side effect. Throw NotImplementedException for BuildRelativePath/OpenWrite/GetSize (unused by the controller's read/delete paths).

Add a `static ExcelExportJob SeedJob(ApplicationDbContext db, ExcelExportJobStatus status, string? filePath)` helper that creates an ExcelExportJob with a fresh Guid, ReportKey/FilterHash set (both are IsRequired in OnModelCreating, so populate them), the given Status and FilePath, adds it, calls SaveChanges, and returns it.
Write `Download_returns_NotFound_for_unknown_id`: build controller over an empty db, call `await controller.Download(Guid.NewGuid())`, assert `Assert.IsType<NotFoundResult>(result)`.

Write `Download_returns_Conflict_when_status_is_not_Completed`: seed a job with Status=Queued and a FilePath, call Download(job.Id), assert `Assert.IsType<ConflictObjectResult>(result)` (the controller returns `Conflict(string)`).

Write `Download_returns_410_Gone_when_file_missing`: seed a Completed job whose FilePath is NOT added to FakeFileStore.ExistingPaths, call Download, assert the result is an `ObjectResult` with `StatusCode == StatusCodes.Status410Gone` (the controller uses `StatusCode(StatusCodes.Status410Gone, ...)`).

Write `Download_returns_File_when_completed_and_exists`: seed a Completed job, add its FilePath to FakeFileStore.ExistingPaths, call Download, assert `Assert.IsType<FileStreamResult>(result)` and that `file.ContentType == StreamingExcelWriter.ContentType` and `file.FileDownloadName == job.FileName`.
Write `Delete_returns_NotFound_for_unknown_id`: empty db, `await controller.Delete(Guid.NewGuid())`, assert `Assert.IsType<NotFoundResult>(result)`.

Write `Delete_returns_NoContent_and_removes_row_and_file`: seed any job (e.g. Completed with a FilePath added to ExistingPaths), call Delete(job.Id), assert `Assert.IsType<NoContentResult>(result)`, assert `await db.ExcelExportJobs.FindAsync(job.Id) is null` (row gone), and assert the FakeFileStore.DeletedPaths contains the job.FilePath (best-effort file delete happened).

Write the policy-asserting test `Any_authenticated_user_can_download_and_delete_any_job_shared_visibility` with an XML doc / inline comment stating this pins the INTENTIONAL shared-visibility model (ExcelExportController.cs:15-18, 33) and the currently-unguarded delete-any-job capability flagged by the audit. Seed a job with RequestedByUserName='userA', build the controller with NO per-user owner claim (or a different principal), and assert Download returns FileStreamResult and Delete returns NoContent — documenting that ownership is NOT enforced today. If the team later decides exports should be owner-scoped, this test is the single place that will fail and force a conscious change.

Set up ControllerContext on each controller instance so `[Authorize]`/User access is safe even though it is not exercised in unit invocation: `controller.ControllerContext = new ControllerContext { HttpContext = new DefaultHttpContext { User = ReportTestUser.AuthenticatedPrincipal } };` reusing the existing internal ReportTestUser (Backend.Tests/ReportTestUser.cs). Wrap this in a `static ExcelExportController NewController(ApplicationDbContext db, IExcelExportFileStore store)` helper.
Run `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj` from the repo root and confirm all new ExcelExportController tests pass alongside the existing suite.
verification: From the repo root run: dotnet test "Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj" --filter FullyQualifiedName~ExcelExportControllerTests — all 7 tests should pass.
Run the full suite once: dotnet test "Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj" — confirm no regressions in the existing Report tests.

Regression-guard sanity check: temporarily comment out the `if (job.Status != ExcelExportJobStatus.Completed) return Conflict(...)` guard or the `!_fileStore.Exists(...)` guard in ExcelExportController.cs and re-run; the corresponding Conflict / 410 test must now FAIL, proving the tests actually pin the branch. Revert the edit afterward.
Confirm grep for ExcelExportController in Backend.Tests now returns the new file: grep -rl ExcelExportController "Backend.Tests".
pitfalls: Do NOT add ExcelExportController to ReportTestHelper.ControllerTypes — that harness filters to namespace 'Backend.Controllers.Report' and constructs controllers with TradeNetDbContext; ExcelExportController lives in Backend.Controllers and needs ApplicationDbContext + IExcelExportFileStore, so it must stay a standalone test class.; EF InMemory enforces required properties at SaveChanges: ReportKey and FilterHash are marked IsRequired in ApplicationDbContext.OnModelCreating, so SeedJob must set both or SaveChanges throws.; Give each test a unique UseInMemoryDatabase name (the nameof(...) pattern shown) — InMemory databases are keyed by name and shared process-wide, so reusing a name leaks seeded rows between tests.; FileStreamResult exposes the download file name as FileDownloadName (not FileName) and content type as ContentType — assert against those property names.; If the team's intent is actually owner-scoped exports (not shared), the policy test will pass today but documents the WRONG model — flag with the product owner before merging; the test's value is making the shared-vs-owner decision explicit, not silently ratifying it.; Delete's file removal is best-effort (try/catch in the controller), so assert on the FakeFileStore.DeletedPaths recording rather than expecting any exception behavior.

approach: Add deterministic, in-memory unit tests that exercise the pure correctness logic (ReportAggregationService group keys / distinct-licence counts / sums / TOTAL footer and ReportUsdConversionService FX math) with test-owned inputs and expected values, then replace the externally-coupled, vacuous smoke assertions (totalCount>=0 and the hardcoded shared-DB counts) with self-seeded EF InMemory fixtures so expected values are owned by the tests and assert on actual field values.
steps: In Backend.Tests, create a new file ReportAggregationServiceTests.cs (namespace Backend.Tests). It needs no DB. Build a small List<AggregateSourceRow> by hand and call ReportAggregationService.Aggregate(rows, dimension, includeSakhan) (static, in API.Service.Reports). Assert on real ReportAggregateResult fields, not just counts.

Add a NoOfLicences distinct-count test: feed two rows with the same LicenceNo and one with a different LicenceNo in the same group key (e.g. dimension=Section, same SectionName/Currency), and assert the single resulting group has NoOfLicences==2 (distinct, OrdinalIgnoreCase) and TotalValue == the sum of all three Amount values. This is exactly the group-key + distinct-count math the smoke tests cannot see.

Add a grouping/ordering test: feed rows spanning two SectionName values with mixed Amount/Currency, assert the output has the expected number of groups, that SectionName/Currency keys map to the right TotalValue, and that the list is ordered (Aggregate calls Order: OrderBy SectionName then Currency, OrdinalIgnoreCase). This guards 'wrong group key' and 'wrong sum' regressions.

Add a ColumnTotals (TOTAL footer) test: call ReportAggregationService.CreatePagedResult(rows, dimension, includeSakhan, new ReportQueryRequest{PageSize=10}, includeColumnTotals: true). Assert result.ColumnTotals is not null and result.ColumnTotals["noOfLicences"] == total distinct licences across ALL groups and result.ColumnTotals["totalValue"] == sum of every Amount. For dimension=Daily also assert the "totalUSDValue" key is present. This directly covers the 'missing TOTAL row' defect class.

Add a Daily-specific ColumnTotals key test: with dimension=ReportAggregateDimension.Daily and includeColumnTotals: true, assert result.ColumnTotals.ContainsKey("totalUSDValue") is true, and with a non-Daily dimension assert it is false — locking in BuildColumnTotals' Daily branch (ReportAggregationService.cs:237-240).

Create a new file ReportUsdConversionServiceTests.cs (namespace Backend.Tests). Use ReportTestHelper.CreateInMemoryDbContext($"Usd_{Guid.NewGuid():N}") (EF InMemory is already referenced in Backend.Tests.csproj). Seed db.Currencies (Currency{Id, Code="USD"} and Code="KRW"/"JPY"/another) and db.ExchangeRates (ExchangeRate{CurrencyId, Date, Rate, Month, Year}) with KNOWN rates, SaveChanges, then build a List<ReportAggregateResult> with Date="2024-01-15" (yyyy-MM-dd), a Currency, and a TotalValue, and call await ReportUsdConversionService.FillDailyUsdValuesAsync(db, groups).

In that file add three assertions matching the documented formula in ReportUsdConversionService.cs: (a) USD pass-through — Currency="USD" gives TotalUSDValue == decimal.Round(TotalValue,4); (b) general currency — TotalUSDValue == round(TotalValue * (currencyRate/usdRate), 4); (c) per-hundred currency — for "KRW"/"JPY" the factor is additionally /100 (assert that branch). Also add a missing-rate test: a date/currency with no ExchangeRate row falls back to factor 1 (the legacy '== null ? 1' default), and a usdRate==0 case returns null.

Replace the vacuous assertion in ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests.cs:33. The Report_post_endpoint_generates_seeded_database_page theory asserts totalCount >= 0, which is true for any int. Either delete this theory (the wiring is already covered by Report_post_endpoint_returns_empty_page_for_empty_authenticated_fixture and the new unit tests) or change it into a self-seeded test that owns its data (see next step). Keep the output.WriteLine diagnostic only if you keep a real assertion.

Convert Representative_report_modules_match_seeded_database_row_counts away from the external, mutable TradeNetDBTest. Stop using ReportTestHelper.CreateTradeNetDbTestDbContext and the hardcoded RepresentativeCounts (MemberRegistration==52, CompanyProfile==3, etc. at ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests.cs:52-75). Instead seed one representative controller's underlying entities into an EF InMemory context via ReportTestHelper.CreateInMemoryDbContext, run InvokePostAsync against that context, and assert GetApiResultTotalCount equals the count YOU seeded (e.g. seed 3 rows -> expect 3) plus at least one field-value assertion by reading apiResult.Data. Expected values are then owned by the test, not the external DB.

If keeping any test that talks to the live TradeNetDBTest, gate it behind a [Trait("Category","RequiresSeededDb")] or a Skip when TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_CONNECTION_STRING is unset, so CI green no longer depends on an external mutable database. The deterministic unit tests from steps 1-7 become the actual regression net.

Run `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj` and confirm the new ReportAggregationServiceTests and ReportUsdConversionServiceTests pass and that deliberately breaking a sum/group-key/TOTAL in ReportAggregationService makes them fail (negative check).
verification: Run: dotnet test "Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj" --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ReportAggregationServiceTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReportUsdConversionServiceTests" — all new tests pass with no DB/SQL Server dependency.

Negative check: temporarily change ReportAggregationService.cs line 117 (TotalValue = group.Sum(...)) to group.Count() or change line 233 (noOfLicences total) — confirm the new aggregation tests now FAIL, proving they actually catch wrong-sum / missing-TOTAL regressions (revert after).
Negative check: temporarily flip the factor in ReportUsdConversionService.cs:139 to usdRate/currencyRate — confirm FillDailyUsdValues_uses_cbm_factor test fails (revert after).

Confirm CI no longer depends on the external TradeNetDBTest: run the full suite with TRADENET_REPORT_TEST_CONNECTION_STRING unset and the live DB unreachable — the deterministic aggregation/USD tests still run and pass; the old hardcoded-count theory is gone or skipped.
Confirm `git grep -n "totalCount >= 0"` returns no remaining vacuous assertion in ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests.cs.
pitfalls: "ReportUsdConversionService.FillDailyUsdValuesAsync queries db.ExchangeRates and db.Currencies, so its test MUST seed both tables; EF InMemory ignores the SQL join semantics but evaluates the LINQ in memory, so the join on CurrencyId/Code works. Seed Currency.Code exactly as the service compares (it does currencyCodes.Contains(currency.Code) and matches USD case-insensitively). Round expected values to 4 decimals because the service calls decimal.Round(..., 4). For KRW/JPY remember the factor is additionally divided by 100 (ReportUsdConversionService.cs:140-143) — do not seed those as ordinary currencies in the general-factor test. Check the real namespace of Currency/ExchangeRate (Model/TradeNet/*.cs — likely API.Model.TradeNet) and adjust the using. When converting Representative_report_modules_match_seeded_database_row_counts to a self-seeded fixture, the controller's Query needs the underlying source entities/views populated; some reports read SQL views (Vw* DbSets) that EF InMemory can populate as regular sets, but a few use raw stored-proc SQL (sp_* via FromSql) which EF InMemory cannot execute — for those, keep them out of the in-memory fixture and rely on the new ReportAggregationService unit tests instead of forcing a fake. Do not delete the wiring-only smoke tests (Report_controller_is_authorized..., Report_post_endpoint_returns_empty_page...) — they still serve a purpose; only strengthen the vacuous assertions."

approach: Delete the form-handler console.log calls that dump submitted credentials in SignIn/SignUp/PasswordReset, and add a defense-in-depth `esbuild.drop` in vite.config.ts so any stray console/debugger statement is stripped from production builds (the project minifies with the default esbuild, not terser, so use the esbuild key — not terser).
steps: In Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignIn.tsx, in the `onFinish` handler, delete line 39 `console.log('Success:', values);` (keep `setLoading(true);` and the rest of the body). Then in `onFinishFailed` (lines 63-65) remove `console.log('Failed:', errorInfo);` — if that leaves the function empty, keep an empty body `const onFinishFailed = () => {};` or drop the `errorInfo` param to avoid an unused-var lint error.

In Frontend/src/pages/authentication/SignUp.tsx, in `onFinish` delete line 46 `console.log('Success:', values);`, and in `onFinishFailed` (lines 59-61) delete line 60 `console.log('Failed:', errorInfo);`. SignUp is unwired demo scaffolding (no backend call, 5s setTimeout navigate, social buttons) so this is hygiene; still remove because the password is in `values`.

In Frontend/src/pages/authentication/PasswordReset.tsx, in `onFinish` delete line 33 `console.log('Success:', values);` and in `onFinishFailed` (lines 46-48) delete line 47 `console.log('Failed:', errorInfo);`. (Note per verifier: this form only contains an email field, not a password — still remove for consistency and to avoid logging the user's email.)

Add a build-time safety net in Frontend/vite.config.ts: add a top-level `esbuild: { drop: ['console', 'debugger'] }` option. The project has no `terser` dependency and no `build.minify` override, so Vite minifies production builds with esbuild — the correct key is `esbuild.drop`, NOT `build.terserOptions`/`build.minify: 'terser'`. This strips console.* (including the `console.log(ex)` at AuthContext.tsx:62 and any future strays) from `vite build` output while leaving them in `vite` dev.

Optional but recommended given this is a government system: also delete `console.log(ex);` at Frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx:62 (in the login catch block) — the axios exception object can include the request config/body, i.e. the password — or replace it with a non-sensitive message. The esbuild.drop in step 4 already neutralizes it in production, but removing it eliminates the leak in dev/preview too.
Rebuild and grep the output to confirm no console calls remain (see verification).
verification: Static check: `cd Frontend && grep -rn "console.log" src/pages/authentication/ src/context/AuthContext.tsx` returns no matches.
Build check: `cd Frontend && npm run build` then `grep -rn 'console.log' dist/assets/*.js` returns nothing — confirms esbuild.drop stripped all console calls from the production bundle.

Runtime check: run `npm run preview` (production build), open DevTools console, log in via the SignIn form, and confirm NO 'Success:' / 'Failed:' object (with email/password) is printed to the console.
Lint check: `cd Frontend && npm run lint` (or `npx eslint src/pages/authentication`) passes with no unused-variable errors after removing the `errorInfo`/`values` references.
pitfalls: If you only delete the console.log but leave the handler param referenced nowhere, ESLint (no-unused-vars / @typescript-eslint) may fail the build — drop the now-unused `errorInfo` param (use `const onFinishFailed = () => {}`) as shown. Do NOT switch the minifier to terser or add `build.minify: 'terser'`/`build.terserOptions.compress.drop_console`: terser is not installed and that would either error or pull in an unnecessary dep; the esbuild.drop key is the correct path for this repo. esbuild.drop only affects `vite build`, not the dev server — so still remove the source-level console.log calls (steps 1-3) rather than relying solely on the build config. Removing AuthContext.tsx:62's `console.log(ex)` removes your only login-failure breadcrumb in dev; if you rely on it, replace with a sanitized log (e.g. log `(ex as AxiosError)?.response?.status`) instead of the whole exception.

approach: Add hardening response headers at the hosting layer: ship a custom nginx config in the Frontend Docker image that sets a strict Content-Security-Policy plus X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Referrer-Policy, scoped to the origins this SPA actually uses (API/image host, QR host, EMQX MQTT broker over wss, Google Fonts). Add a CSP <meta> tag in index.html only as a fallback for the directives meta tags support.
steps: Create Frontend/nginx.conf with the server block in the patch. Keep the `try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;` SPA fallback (the React Router app needs it) and the four `add_header ... always;` lines. Use `always` so headers are emitted on error responses too.

Scope connect-src/img-src to the REAL production origins. The codebase defaults in Frontend/src/config.ts are baseUrl=https://localhost:8000/api/, imageUrl=https://localhost:8000/Image/, qrUrl=https://uatapi.ecomreg.gov.mm/QR/, all overridable via VITE_BASE_URL/VITE_IMAGE_URL/VITE_QR_URL at build time. Before deploying, replace https://uatapi.ecomreg.gov.mm in the CSP with the actual prod API origin used in the build's VITE_BASE_URL (origin only — scheme+host+port, no path). Keep wss://broker.emqx.io:8084 (the MQTT broker hardcoded in Frontend/src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx:43) and https://api.qrserver.com (hardcoded in Frontend/src/pages/certificate/Certificate.tsx:54) until those are brought in-house.

Wire the config into the image: in Frontend/dockerfile, add `COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf` immediately after the existing `COPY --from=0 /app/build/ /usr/share/nginx/html` line (stock nginx:alpine includes a default.conf at that path, so this overwrites it).

Note a pre-existing build-output mismatch you'll hit while testing: package.json build script is `tsc && vite build` and vite.config.ts sets no `build.outDir`, so Vite emits to `dist/`, but the dockerfile copies `/app/build/`. If the image is currently building empty, also set `build: { outDir: 'build' }` in vite.config.ts (or change the dockerfile COPY to `/app/dist/`). This is orthogonal to the CSP work but blocks local verification.

Add the fallback CSP <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"> tag in Frontend/index.html right after the <meta charset> on line 4. Omit frame-ancestors, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options and Referrer-Policy from the meta tag — those are ignored in meta form and only work as real response headers from nginx.

Verify Google Fonts still load: the page pulls the stylesheet from https://fonts.googleapis.com and font files from https://fonts.gstatic.com (Frontend/index.html lines 34-39), so style-src must include fonts.googleapis.com and font-src must include fonts.gstatic.com. 'unsafe-inline' is kept on style-src because Ant Design 5 injects runtime inline <style> tags; do not drop it without migrating to a nonce/hash strategy or the UI will lose styling.

Decide on the demo/template image hosts: Certificate.tsx and a few template pages reference api.dicebear.com, i.pravatar.cc, images.unsplash.com, upload.wikimedia.org. With the strict img-src above these will be blocked. Either remove the leftover template pages, or if any are real, add those hosts to img-src. Confirm none are on production routes before tightening.
verification: Build and run the image: `docker compose -f Frontend/docker-compose.production.yml up -d --build`, then `curl -sI http://localhost:3000/ | grep -iE 'content-security-policy|x-frame-options|x-content-type-options|referrer-policy'` — all four headers should appear.

Load the app in a browser and open DevTools Console: confirm no CSP violation errors for the API XHR/fetch calls, the EMQX wss connection in Chat, fonts, and any in-use images. Any 'Refused to connect/load' error names a missing allowed origin to add.
Click-jacking check: try framing the app in a test page `<iframe src="http://localhost:3000">` — it must render blank/blocked due to frame-ancestors 'none' + X-Frame-Options: DENY.

Confirm the SPA still routes: deep-link to a non-root path (e.g. http://localhost:3000/report/...) and verify it returns index.html (200) rather than nginx 404, proving the try_files fallback survived.
Run the repo's existing security review (`/security-review`) or re-run the audit to confirm the no-content-security-policy finding clears.
pitfalls: Order: the API origin in connect-src/img-src MUST be set to the actual prod VITE_BASE_URL origin before deploy — leaving https://localhost:8000 or the wrong host will break all API calls in production (every report request goes through Frontend/src/services/AxiosInstance.ts which prepends envConfig.baseUrl). CSP is fail-closed, so an overly strict policy silently breaks features rather than erroring loudly — validate in a staging build first. Do NOT remove 'unsafe-inline' from style-src; AntD's CSS-in-JS needs it and the whole UI goes unstyled without it. The index.html meta tag cannot enforce frame-ancestors/X-Frame-Options/nosniff/Referrer-Policy, so do not treat it as a substitute for the nginx headers — it's only a fallback for the script/connect/img directives. Rollback is trivial: revert the dockerfile COPY line and rebuild; the meta tag can stay or be removed independently. Note this is defense-in-depth (no known XSS sink exists in this codebase), not a fix for an active vulnerability — do not let strictness experiments block the deploy.

approach: Stop using the free public `broker.emqx.io` test broker and the single global topic. Move the broker URL + credentials into config/env, connect over authenticated TLS, subscribe/publish on a per-room topic derived from the route `id`, and drop `retain` on the control message so room ids are no longer persisted on a shared public broker.
steps: Edit Frontend/src/config.ts: add `mqttUrl`, `mqttUsername`, `mqttPassword` keys to the `envConfig` object, each reading from `import.meta.env.VITE_MQTT_*` with a non-public fallback (do NOT fall back to broker.emqx.io). Use `wss://localhost:8084/mqtt` as the dev default.

In Frontend/src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx line 39, change `const topic = 'chat-app-room2';` to a per-room topic: `const topic = \`chat/room/${FixedRoomNo}\`;` so each conversation has its own topic instead of one shared global topic. Move/keep this line after `FixedRoomNo` is defined (line 31) so it's in scope.

In ChatBox.tsx line 43, replace `mqtt.connect('wss://broker.emqx.io:8084/mqtt')` with `mqtt.connect(envConfig.mqttUrl, { username: envConfig.mqttUsername, password: envConfig.mqttPassword, protocolVersion: 5, clean: true })`. `envConfig` is already imported on line 18, so no new import is needed.

In ChatBox.tsx `sendTextMessage` (lines 84-87) change the publish options from `{ retain: true }` to `{ retain: false, qos: 1 }` so the room id is not persisted on the broker for future/foreign subscribers.
Apply the identical change in `sendImageMessage` (lines 107-109) — the verifierNotes call this out as a second publish block with the same `retain:true` bug.

Harden the message handler (lines 50-63): instead of refetching on ANY valid JSON, only refetch when the parsed payload equals the current room — e.g. `const payload = JSON.parse(messageBuffer.toString()); if (payload !== FixedRoomNo) return;` before the `axiosInstance.get`. Now that the topic is per-room this is mostly defense-in-depth, but it removes the cheap notification-spoof vector.

Provision the private broker out-of-band and document the env vars: add `VITE_MQTT_URL`, `VITE_MQTT_USERNAME`, `VITE_MQTT_PASSWORD` to the deployment env (and a committed `.env.example` since no .env is currently checked in). The broker must require auth, run over TLS (wss), and enforce per-user ACLs so a client can only subscribe/publish to its own `chat/room/<id>` topics. Treat the WebSocket username/password as low-trust (it ships to the browser) — rely on the server-side ACL/topic scoping for the real authorization, not the client credentials.

Recommended hardening on the backend: add `[Authorize]` to Backend/Controllers/ChatController.cs (currently it has none) and verify the authenticated user is a participant of `room` in both `Get` and `Post`, so the authoritative chat history fetch cannot be abused even if someone reaches the topic. This is the durable mitigation for the data-exposure dimension.
verification: Run `grep -rn 'broker.emqx.io' Frontend/src` and confirm zero matches.
Run `grep -rn "retain: true" Frontend/src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx` and confirm zero matches (both publish blocks updated).

In the browser DevTools Network tab (WS filter), open a chat and confirm the WebSocket connects to your private VITE_MQTT_URL host (not broker.emqx.io) and that the upgrade carries the configured credentials / the connection is rejected without them.
Confirm the subscribed/published MQTT topic is `chat/room/<roomId>` and differs between two different conversations (open two rooms, inspect the SUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH frames).

Send a message and confirm the other participant's ChatBox refetches via `GET chat?room=...`; then publish a foreign/garbage JSON value to the room topic and confirm it does NOT trigger a refetch (validates the room-equality guard).
After connecting and disconnecting, reconnect a fresh client and confirm NO retained room-id message is delivered on subscribe (validates retain:false).
`cd Frontend && npm run build` (or `tsc --noEmit`) compiles with no type errors from the new mqtt connect options.
pitfalls: Ordering: define/compute the per-room topic AFTER `FixedRoomNo` is set (line 31) and reference it inside the connect effect — `topic` is used in the `useEffect` (subscribe) and both send functions, so keep it module-scoped within the component. The connect `useEffect` has an empty dep array `[]`; if you derive `topic` from `FixedRoomNo`/`id`, add `FixedRoomNo` (or `id`) to the dependency array so navigating between rooms re-subscribes to the correct topic, and ensure the cleanup `client.end()` still runs to avoid leaking connections. mqtt.js v5 accepts `username`/`password`/`protocolVersion` in `IClientOptions`, so no API change is needed, but note browser-shipped credentials are not a real secret — the broker ACL is the actual control. Rollback is a straight revert of the two files; no data migration. Do NOT ship while the broker is still the public default fallback — if `VITE_MQTT_URL` is unset in an environment it will hit `wss://localhost:8084/mqtt` and chat will simply not connect (fail-closed), which is the intended safe behavior.
estimatedTime: Frontend code change: ~45 min. Provisioning + ACL-configuring the private authenticated broker (infra): 0.5–1 day. Optional ChatController [Authorize] + participant check: ~30 min.

approach: Remove the static `import * as XLSX from 'xlsx'` at the top of the shared BasicTable and load SheetJS on demand via `await import('xlsx')` inside the client export path. This splits the ~864KB dependency out of the main bundle into a lazily-fetched chunk, so it only downloads when a user actually clicks Excel on a table that has no server-side `onExcel` (today only UserList.tsx).
steps: Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx.
Delete the static import on line 19: `import * as XLSX from 'xlsx';`. Leave the `FileExcelOutlined` import on line 18 untouched.

Convert `exportClientTableToExcel` (sync arrow fn at lines 236-244) to async and dynamically import xlsx inside it, replacing the module-level `XLSX` references with the dynamically imported namespace.

In `handleExcel` (lines 246-262) await the fallback branch: change `exportClientTableToExcel(); return;` to `await exportClientTableToExcel(); return;`. handleExcel is already `async`, so no signature change is needed.
Optional polish: wrap the client export body in try/catch and surface failure via the existing `setError('Failed to generate Excel file.')` to match the onExcel branch.
From Frontend/, run `npx tsc --noEmit`, then `npm run build`, and confirm Vite emits a separate `xlsx-*.js` async chunk rather than bundling SheetJS into the main entry.
verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes (the `await import('xlsx')` namespace resolves the same `utils`/`writeFile` types as the previous namespace import).
`npm run build` produces a distinct async chunk for xlsx: inspect dist/assets and confirm a separate `xlsx-*.js` file exists and the main entry chunk shrank by roughly the SheetJS size.

Run the app, open Users (UserList.tsx — the only BasicTable consumer without onExcel), open devtools Network, click Excel, and confirm the xlsx chunk is fetched on click (not at initial page load) and the .xlsx downloads correctly.
Open any GenericReportPage report and confirm Excel still routes through the server job queue (POST to config.excelRoute) and no xlsx chunk loads for that flow.
pitfalls: `await import('xlsx')` returns the module namespace object, so `XLSX.utils` / `XLSX.writeFile` work unchanged — do not add `.default`. Keep awaiting `exportClientTableToExcel()` in handleExcel so error handling/loading state stays correct. Keep `xlsx` (^0.18.5) in package.json dependencies — it is still used, just deferred. No backend or data-migration impact; rollback is reverting the two edits.

approach: Add a `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` map to `Frontend/vite.config.ts` that splits heavy, rarely-changing vendors (React/Redux, antd + icons/charts, xlsx, firebase/mqtt, date libs) into their own chunks so they cache independently of app code, and wire in `rollup-plugin-visualizer` (gated behind an env flag) so bundle composition can be attributed per dependency.
steps: Install the visualizer as a dev dependency from the Frontend directory: `cd "/Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Frontend" && npm install -D rollup-plugin-visualizer` (it is NOT currently in node_modules; this adds it under devDependencies in package.json).

Edit `Frontend/vite.config.ts`. Add two imports at the top: `import { visualizer } from 'rollup-plugin-visualizer';` and `import { loadEnv } from 'vite';` (loadEnv lets you read an opt-in flag without crashing if it is unset).

Change the default export to the function form `defineConfig(({ mode }) => { ... })` so you can read env and conditionally enable the visualizer. Inside it call `const env = loadEnv(mode, process.cwd(), '');`.

In `plugins`, keep `react()` and append the visualizer only when opted in: `...(env.ANALYZE === 'true' ? [visualizer({ filename: 'dist/stats.html', gzipSize: true, brotliSize: true, template: 'treemap' })] : [])`. Gating it keeps normal `npm run build` output clean and avoids opening a browser tab in CI.

Add a `build` section with `chunkSizeWarningLimit: 1000` and `rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` as a function that routes node_modules deps into vendor chunks (Vite 6 / Rollup 4 supports the function form). Group by package: react/react-dom/react-router/redux into `vendor-react`, antd + @ant-design/* into `vendor-antd`, xlsx into `vendor-xlsx`, firebase + mqtt into `vendor-realtime`, dayjs/moment into `vendor-date`; everything else under node_modules falls through to a generic `vendor` chunk.

Add a convenience script to `Frontend/package.json` scripts: `"analyze": "cross-env ANALYZE=true npm run build"` (or on the existing macOS/zsh dev box simply run `ANALYZE=true npm run build` — cross-env is optional and only needed for Windows CI).

Run `npm run build` once to confirm chunks are emitted and the single ~2.5MB blob is now split; run `ANALYZE=true npm run build` to generate `dist/stats.html` and open it to attribute size per dependency.
verification: From Frontend/, run `npm run build` and confirm it succeeds and the `dist/assets/` output now contains separate `vendor-react-*.js`, `vendor-antd-*.js`, `vendor-xlsx-*.js`, etc. instead of one ~2.5MB bundle (e.g. `ls -la dist/assets`).
Run `ANALYZE=true npm run build` and open `Frontend/dist/stats.html` in a browser; verify the treemap renders and lets you attribute size per dependency (antd, xlsx, firebase visibly dominate).
Confirm a plain `npm run build` does NOT emit `dist/stats.html` (visualizer stays opt-in).
Sanity-check caching: build, change one app source file, rebuild, and confirm the `vendor-*` chunk filenames/hashes are unchanged while the app/entry chunk hash changes.
pitfalls: "`npm run build` is `tsc && vite build`, so the converted function-form config must be valid TypeScript — keep the explicit `return { ... }`. The `manualChunks` function form is supported in Vite 6 (Rollup 4), confirmed local vite 6.3.5. Order of the regex checks matters: test react-router/react-redux/@reduxjs before a generic `react` match, and rc-* (antd's internal deps) goes with vendor-antd. Do not over-split: putting every package in its own chunk causes many small HTTP requests; the grouped buckets above are deliberate. `ANALYZE=true npm run build` only works in bash/zsh; for Windows/CI use the `cross-env` script. Rollback is trivial: revert vite.config.ts and remove rollup-plugin-visualizer from devDependencies — no runtime or data impact since this is build-time only."

steps: Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx. At line 497, change the Ant Design Pagination prop `pageSizeOptions={[10, 20, 50, 100, 1000]}` to `pageSizeOptions={[10, 20, 50, 100]}`. If a caller can pass `initialPageSize` or restore a saved query, clamp it so a stale 1000 can't slip past the dropdown — e.g. wrap incoming values with `Math.min(value, 100)` where `initialPageSize` (line 135) and `setPageSize` (lines 501, 508) are assigned. For the standalone CompanyProfile state, do the same where `setPageSize` is called (lines 613, 617).

Repeat on the Company Profile screen (CompanyProfile.tsx) and confirm the same dropdown no longer offers 1000.

Switch to 100 rows on a wide report and confirm the tab stays responsive (no multi-second freeze). Then click the Excel button to confirm the server export still returns the full dataset for bulk needs.
grep to prove no 1000 option remains: rg "pageSizeOptions=\\[10, 20, 50, 100, 1000\\]" Frontend/src should return zero matches.
TypeScript build passes: npm run build (or tsc --noEmit) in Frontend/ with no errors from the edited files.
pitfalls: Two independent copies of the pagination control exist — BasicTable.tsx (line 497) and CompanyProfile.tsx (line 607); changing only one leaves the other exposed, so both must be edited. Removing 1000 is user-visible behavior: anyone who used "1000" to eyeball/print a full report must now use the Excel export instead — call this out in release notes. If any saved/URL-persisted query still carries pageSize=1000, the Pagination component may render with no matching dropdown entry; the optional Math.min clamp prevents that. steps: In Frontend/src/Report/reportNavItems.tsx, export a reusable item-builder so SideNav can rebuild a filtered tree. Add an exported function `buildReportNavItems(filter?: string)` that returns the same structure currently produced inline at lines 223-242, but filters report items by `config.title` (case-insensitive `includes`). Keep `reportNavItems` exported as `buildReportNavItems()` for callers that want the full tree. Reuse the existing `createReportItem`, `reportCategoryDefinitions`, `getReportCategoryKey`, and `reportConfigList`.

Inside `buildReportNavItems`, when a non-empty filter is supplied: (a) keep the top-level 'Exports' item only if 'exports' matches the filter; (b) for each category, filter its `reportConfigList` children by `config.title.toLowerCase().includes(q)` and drop categories whose children become empty (the existing `.filter((category) => category.children.length > 0)` already does this); (c) filter the trailing uncategorized configs the same way.

In Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx, import `useMemo` from 'react' and `Input` from 'antd' (and optionally `SearchOutlined` from '@ant-design/icons'). Replace the static `const items: MenuProps['items'] = reportNavItems;` (line 17) with a stateful query: add `const [query, setQuery] = useState('');` inside the component and compute `const items = useMemo(() => buildReportNavItems(query), [query]);`. Update the import on line 11 from `reportNavItems` to `buildReportNavItems`.

When `query` is non-empty, force the matching categories open so results are visible without manual expansion: compute the open keys from the filtered tree. e.g. `const filteredOpenKeys = useMemo(() => query ? items.filter(i => 'children' in (i ?? {})).map(i => i!.key as string) : openKeys, [query, items, openKeys]);` and pass `openKeys={filteredOpenKeys}` to the `<Menu>` (line 93). Keep `onOpenChange` for the non-search case.

Render the search box directly above the `<Menu>` (inside the `<ConfigProvider>` at lines 76-98, before `<Menu>` at line 88): `<Input allowClear prefix={<SearchOutlined />} placeholder="Search reports" value={query} onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)} style={{ margin: '0.5rem 1rem', width: 'calc(100% - 2rem)' }} />`.
Remove the debug log at SideNav.tsx line 32: delete `console.log('click ', e);` from the `onClick` handler, leaving the `if (isMobile) { setCollapse(true); }` behavior intact.

In Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx, remove the two collapse-debug logs inside the `useEffect(..., [collapsed])`: delete `console.log(collapsed);` at line 115 and line 122. Leave the surrounding `if (!collapsed) { ... } else { ... }` DOM logic untouched. (Optionally keep the 401 log at line 139, or downgrade it; it is not part of this finding.)

verification: Run the Vite dev server (npm run dev in Frontend/) and open the report sidebar. Type 'licence' in the new search box: the menu should collapse to only matching report items with their parent categories auto-expanded; clear it (allowClear X) to restore all 134 reports.

grep to confirm cleanup: `grep -rn "console.log" Frontend/src/layouts/app/SideNav.tsx Frontend/src/layouts/app/App.tsx` returns no menu-click or collapse-debug lines (the only remaining hit may be the intentional 401 logout log).

The `openKeys`/`onOpenChange` logic in SideNav uses controlled state; when forcing categories open during search via filteredOpenKeys, do not also call setOpenKeys in a render path or you'll get a render loop — derive it with useMemo only. approach: Migrate the Frontend lint setup from the dead legacy `.eslintrc.cjs` (incompatible with ESLint 9) to a flat `eslint.config.js`, fix the `lint` npm script to drop the unsupported `--ext` flag, add the missing `typescript-eslint` meta-package, then wire a real lint step into CI so the gate actually runs.
steps: Add the missing meta package: in Frontend run `npm install -D typescript-eslint@^8.34.0`. (`@typescript-eslint/parser` and `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` 8.34.0 are already in devDependencies, and `@eslint/js` + `globals` are already in node_modules; only the umbrella `typescript-eslint` package is absent — verified it is NOT installed.)

Create Frontend/eslint.config.js as an ESM flat config (the project has `"type": "module"` in package.json, so `.js` is ESM — do NOT use `.cjs`). Use `tseslint.config(...)` from `typescript-eslint`, extend `js.configs.recommended` and `...tseslint.configs.recommended`, set `languageOptions.globals = globals.browser`, register the `react-hooks` and `react-refresh` plugins, spread `reactHooks.configs['recommended-latest'].rules` (this is the flat-config rules object exposed by eslint-plugin-react-hooks@5.2.0), and re-add the existing `'react-refresh/only-export-components': ['warn', { allowConstantExport: true }]` rule that was in .eslintrc.cjs. Append `...storybook.configs['flat/recommended']` (eslint-plugin-storybook@9.0.9 exposes the `flat/recommended` key — confirmed) so the storybook lint rules carried over from the old `plugin:storybook/recommended` extend are preserved.

Set `ignores: ['dist', 'storybook-static', '*.config.js', '*.config.ts']` as the first config object so the config file and build output are not linted (replaces the old `ignorePatterns: ['dist', '.eslintrc.cjs']`). Scope the TS rules block with `files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx}']`.

Edit Frontend/package.json line 11: change the `lint` script from `eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0` to `eslint src --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 0`. The `--ext` flag is removed in ESLint 9 (flat config infers extensions from the `files` globs); narrowing to `src` avoids linting config/build files.
Delete Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs. Leaving it alongside eslint.config.js is harmless to ESLint 9 (flat config takes precedence) but is dead/misleading config — remove it.

Run `npm run lint` once. Expect it to now actually execute and report the backlog (~181 problems: ~172 errors, ~9 warnings per the audit). Triage these in a separate follow-up commit — do NOT mix the large auto-fix/manual-fix churn with this tooling migration. For a quick first pass run `npx eslint src --fix` to clear mechanically-fixable items, then address the remaining `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` (66+ hits), unused vars, missing hook deps, and empty patterns by hand.

Wire lint into CI so the gate is real (the audit verifier confirmed NO workflow currently runs lint). In Frontend/.github/workflows/test.yml, add a step before the `Build application` step (after `Clean install dependencies`): name `Lint`, `run: npm run lint`, with `working-directory` matching where the other steps run. Also bump the stale `node-version` matrix (currently `16.x`/`18.x`) to a version ESLint 9 and Vite 6 support, e.g. `20.x`, since ESLint 9 requires Node >=18.18 and the current matrix would fail install. Confirm the workflow's default working directory points at Frontend (the workflow file lives under Frontend/.github, so paths resolve relative to repo Frontend dir when checked out — verify the runner's cwd, set `defaults.run.working-directory: Frontend` if the repo is checked out at the monorepo root).
verification: From Frontend/, run `npx eslint --print-config src/main.tsx | head` — it should print a resolved flat config (proves eslint.config.js is picked up) instead of the v9 'couldn't find an eslint.config file' error.

Run `npm run lint`; confirm it exits non-zero (code 1) and prints the real backlog of problems, rather than exiting 2 with the 'ESLint couldn't find an eslint.config.(js|mjs|cjs)' message that the broken command produced (reproduced: `npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx` currently errors with exactly that).

Seed a deliberate violation to prove the gate bites: add `const x: any = 1;` to a file under src/, run `npm run lint`, confirm it reports `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` and exits 1; remove the seed and (after triage) confirm a clean exit 0.
After the triage commit, run `npm run lint` and confirm exit 0 with `--max-warnings 0` honored.
Push a branch and confirm the CI `Test` workflow now shows a `Lint` step that runs `npm run lint` and fails the job on lint errors.
pitfalls: Ordering: install `typescript-eslint` and create eslint.config.js BEFORE deleting .eslintrc.cjs and before changing the script, so you never have a window where lint is more broken than now. Because package.json has `"type": "module"`, eslint.config.js is ESM — use `import`/`export default`, not `module.exports`; a CJS-style file will throw at load. Do NOT separate the two churn sources: keep the tooling migration (config + script + CI) in one commit and the ~172 error fixes in a follow-up, so the large diff doesn't bury the migration and can be rolled back independently. `eslint-plugin-react-hooks@5.2.0` flat rules live under `configs['recommended-latest']` (the `recommended` key is the legacy/eslintrc form) — using the wrong key silently applies no rules. `eslint-plugin-storybook` uses the `flat/recommended` key for flat config (the bare `recommended` is legacy). The CI node matrix is `16.x`/`18.x`; ESLint 9 requires Node >=18.18.0, so `16.x` will fail — bump it or the new lint step errors for an unrelated reason. Rollback: restore .eslintrc.cjs and the old script, remove eslint.config.js; the legacy path then works again only via `ESLINT_USE_FLAT_CONFIG=false` (deprecated), so prefer fixing forward.
estimatedTime: Tooling migration + CI wiring: ~1 hour. Triaging the ~172 accumulated lint errors: ~1 day (separate task).
