In `AppendRows<T>` (line 67) replace `_properties ??= GetExportProperties(row.GetType());` with `_accessor ??= ExcelPropertyAccessor.For(row.GetType());` and at line 75 replace `WriteRow(_sheetWriter!, _rowInSheet, _properties!.Select(p => p.GetValue(row)));` with `WriteRow(_sheetWriter!, _rowInSheet, _accessor!.GetValues(row));`.

In `StreamingExcelWriter.StartNewSheet` (line 125) replace `WriteRow(_sheetWriter, HeaderRow, (_properties ?? Array.Empty<PropertyInfo>()).Select(p => (object?)p.Name));` with `WriteRow(_sheetWriter, HeaderRow, (_accessor?.ColumnNames ?? Array.Empty<string>()).Select(name => (object?)name));`. In `Finish` (line 86) replace `_properties ??= Array.Empty<PropertyInfo>();` with `_accessor ??= ExcelPropertyAccessor.Empty;` (add a static `Empty` instance with zero columns to the cache class), or simply guard `StartNewSheet` against a null accessor — keep the empty-sheet behavior unchanged. Delete the now-unused private `GetExportProperties(Type)` helper (lines 146-152).

In `Backend/Service/Reports/ExcelGenerator.cs`: in `CreateWorkbook<T>` (line 53) replace `var properties = GetExportProperties<T>();` with `var accessor = ExcelPropertyAccessor.For(typeof(T));` and pass `accessor` to `WriteWorksheet` instead of `properties` (line 62). Add `using API.Service.ExcelExport;`.

Change `WriteWorksheet<T>(Stream stream, PropertyInfo[] properties, IReadOnlyList<T> rows)` (line 76) to `WriteWorksheet<T>(Stream stream, ExcelPropertyAccessor accessor, IReadOnlyList<T> rows)`. Line 89 header becomes `WriteRow(writer, 1, accessor.ColumnNames.Select(name => (object?)name));` and lines 91-94 become a loop using `accessor.GetValues(rows[rowIndex])` instead of `properties.Select(property => property.GetValue(rows[rowIndex]))`.
Delete the now-unused `GetExportProperties<T>()` helper in `ExcelGenerator.cs` (lines 68-74) and remove `using System.Reflection;` from that file (it is otherwise unused after this change).
Keep `WriteRow`, `WriteCell`, `TryGetNumericValue`, `FormatValue`, and `GetCellReference` untouched in both files — only the value-source changes; the cell formatting path is identical.
verification: Build: `dotnet build` from `Backend/` succeeds with no warnings about unused `System.Reflection` usings.

Run an export through the existing async queue (`ExcelExportController` / `ExcelExportWorker` with `MaxConcurrency=1`) for one of the Fast reports (e.g. sp_ImportLicenceDetailReport_Fast) and open the resulting .xlsx: header row text and every cell value must be byte-for-byte identical to a pre-change export (column order, numeric vs inlineStr cells, date/bool formatting all unchanged).

Verify the streaming multi-sheet path: export a result set that crosses 1,048,576 rows (or temporarily lower MaxRowsPerSheet in a debug build) and confirm the header row still repeats correctly on sheet 2 (driven by ColumnNames, not PropertyInfo).

Optional micro-benchmark: time CreateWorkbook on a ~100k-row, ~40-column fixture before and after; the compiled-getter path should show lower CPU/time. The accessor is built (and JIT-compiled) once per type, then served from the ConcurrentDictionary.
pitfalls: Selection-rule parity is the critical correctness constraint: keep the exact `GetProperties(Public|Instance).Where(GetIndexParameters().Length == 0)` filter and the same property ordering as the old `GetExportProperties`, otherwise columns reorder or disappear. GetProperties order is stable for a given type/runtime, matching prior behavior.; StreamingExcelWriter infers the type from the runtime type of the first non-null boxed row (`row.GetType()`), which may be more derived than `T`; keep using `ExcelPropertyAccessor.For(row.GetType())` there (not `typeof(T)`) to preserve identical column inference. ExcelGenerator uses `typeof(T)` exactly as before.; `Expression.Convert(access, typeof(object))` boxes value-type/nullable property values the same way `PropertyInfo.GetValue` did, so the downstream `WriteCell`/`TryGetNumericValue`/`FormatValue` logic is unaffected. Do not change those methods.; This is a Low-severity CPU optimization (the verifier noted XmlWriter writes, string formatting, DB query, and zip compression are comparable or larger costs); do not expand scope to rewrite the cell-writing or compression paths. No data migration, no API/contract change, fully revertable by restoring the two files and deleting ExcelPropertyAccessor.cs.

approach: Add DataAnnotations to the shared base DTO (ReportQueryRequest) so [ApiController] auto-returns 400s for bad PageSize/PageIndex, and harden the single shared clamp point (ApiResult.NormalizePageSize) to cap at MaxPageSize=1000 so the one uncapped path (ImportLicenceBySectionReportController) is covered without touching ~157 controllers. Date-range validation stays as-is (per-report DTOs have report-specific FromDate/ToDate and live below the base class).
steps: Open Backend/Model/ReportQueryRequest.cs and add validation attributes to the shared base DTO. Add `using System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;` at the top. Put `[Range(1, 1000, ErrorMessage = "PageSize must be between 1 and 1000.")]` on `PageSize` and `[Range(0, int.MaxValue, ErrorMessage = "PageIndex must be zero or greater.")]` on `PageIndex`. Keep `PageSize`'s default of 10 (= 10). Because every report request class (e.g. ImportLicenceDetailReportRequest, line 143) derives from ReportQueryRequest and every controller has [ApiController], invalid values now auto-return ProblemDetails 400 before the action body runs.

Centralize the upper-bound clamp so no report path can exceed it as a defense-in-depth backstop (attribute validation only fires if the client actually sends an out-of-range value through the normal binder; the clamp guarantees the cap regardless). In Backend/Model/APIResult.cs change `NormalizePageSize` (lines 204-207) from `return pageSize <= 0 ? 10 : pageSize;` to clamp to a new `MaxPageSize` constant of 1000, mirroring the constants already in ReportQueryService.cs (lines 10-11) and the _Fast SP files (e.g. sp_ImportLicenceDetailReport_Fast.cs lines 22-23). Add private const fields `DefaultPageSize = 10` and `MaxPageSize = 1000` to ApiResult<T> and use them in NormalizePageSize. This closes the ImportLicenceBySectionReportController path, which calls ApiResult<ReportAggregateResult>.CreatePageFromRows (line 77) -> NormalizePageSize.

Fix the one controller that computes pageSize inline before paging, so its in-memory `Take(pageSize)` slice is also capped. In Backend/Controllers/Report/ImportLicenceBySectionReportController.cs line 71, change `var pageSize = request.PageSize <= 0 ? 10 : request.PageSize;` to also bound the upper end: `var pageSize = request.PageSize <= 0 ? 10 : Math.Min(request.PageSize, 1000);`. (CreatePageFromRows now also clamps, but this avoids materializing an oversized page slice before the call.)

Do NOT remove the existing per-controller `TryCreateReportRequest` date guards (e.g. ImportLicenceDetailReportController.cs lines 108-124). They validate report-specific FromDate/ToDate/cross-field rules (ToDate >= FromDate) that the base ReportQueryRequest does not own; converting those to attributes is a separate, larger refactor and out of scope for this Low finding. The attribute work here is limited to the shared paging fields.
Build the backend to confirm no compile errors: run `dotnet build` from the Backend project directory.
verification: POST to any report endpoint (e.g. /api/ImportLicenceDetailReport) with a valid JWT and a body containing `"pageSize": 999999`. With the attribute in place [ApiController] returns HTTP 400 with a ProblemDetails body whose errors include the PageSize range message; previously it returned 200.
POST the same with `"pageSize": -5` or `"pageIndex": -1` and confirm 400.
POST with a valid body (`"pageSize": 50`) and confirm a normal 200 with at most 50 rows, proving valid requests are unaffected and the default of 10 still applies when pageSize is omitted.

Defense-in-depth check on the previously-uncapped path: POST to /api/ImportLicenceBySectionReport. Even if a request reaches NormalizePageSize/CreatePageFromRows with a large pageSize, the returned ApiResult.PageSize is now <= 1000 and Data length is capped at 1000.
Run `dotnet build` in the Backend project and confirm it compiles with no errors.
pitfalls: PageSize keeps its default of 10, and [Range(1,1000)] permits 10, so omitting pageSize still binds to the default and validates fine — do not set the Range minimum above the default. Apply the NormalizePageSize clamp (step 2) and the controller clamp (step 3) regardless of the attribute work: attribute validation only rejects values that actually arrive through the action's model binding, while the clamp is the backstop that guarantees the 1000 cap for every code path (and matches the existing MaxPageSize=1000 already used in ReportQueryService.cs and the _Fast SP files, so values stay consistent). Per the verifier note, the ImportLicenceBySection rows are already materialized/cached in BuildRowsAsync before paging, so this mitigates forced serialization of an oversized page slice, not a DB-side large read — keep expectations framed that way. Do not delete the per-controller date guards; they cover report-specific fields the base DTO doesn't have. Rollback is trivial: revert the three files; no data migration or config change is involved.

approach: Treat the two contexts per their nature: ApplicationDbContext is app-owned, so add a guarded MigrateAsync() at startup so deploys can't run code ahead of schema; TradeNetDbContext is a read-model over an externally-owned schema (TradeNetDBTest, keyless .ToView mappings), so don't author migrations for it — instead pin the exact re-scaffold command in a script and add a CI/local staleness check that fails when the committed scaffold no longer matches the canonical DB.
steps: Add a guarded migration step for the app-owned context in Backend/Program.cs. After `var app = builder.Build();` (line 97) and before the pipeline config, resolve ApplicationDbContext from a scope and call MigrateAsync(). Guard it behind config so it can be disabled in environments where DBAs apply migrations manually: read `builder.Configuration.GetValue<bool>("Database:MigrateOnStartup")`.

In Backend/appsettings.json add a `"Database": { "MigrateOnStartup": true }` section (and set it to false in appsettings.Development.json or per-environment as the DBA team prefers). Only ApplicationDbContext gets MigrateAsync() — do NOT call Migrate()/EnsureCreated() on TradeNetDbContext, because that context has no migrations and maps a schema this app does not own.

Document and pin the TradeNetDbContext regeneration command. Create Backend/scaffold-tradenet.ps1 (or .sh) containing the exact `dotnet ef dbcontext scaffold` invocation: connection from the `TradeNetDBTest` connection string, provider Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer, `--context TradeNetDbContext --context-dir DBContext --output-dir Model/TradeNet --no-onconfiguring --use-database-names --force`. This is the canonical, repeatable way to refresh the 228-entity read-model (matches the existing API.Model.TradeNet namespace and DBContext/ folder).

Add a scaffold-staleness check the same script can run in --verify mode: re-scaffold into a temp directory, then `git diff --no-index` the generated TradeNetDbContext.cs and Model/TradeNet against the committed copies; exit non-zero on any diff. This is the CI gate the finding asks for — it fails the build when the committed scaffold has drifted from the live TradeNetDB schema.

Because the backend currently has no GitHub Actions workflow (only Frontend/.github/workflows/* exist) and deploys via deploy.ps1 to P:\WEBSITES, wire the verify step into deploy.ps1 right after `dotnet build` (around line 31): invoke the scaffold script in --verify mode against the canonical DB and abort the publish if it reports drift. If a backend CI pipeline is later added, run the same script there instead.

Add an integration test in Backend.Tests that asserts the scaffolded TradeNetDbContext model matches the real database. The project already has ReportSqlServerFixture.cs and ReportQueryTranslationTests.cs; add a test that opens a TradeNetDbContext against the canonical connection and calls `db.Database.GetService<IRelationalDatabaseCreator>().HasTables()` plus iterates each entity type and runs a `Take(0)` query (or `context.Model.GetEntityTypes()` mapped to a trivial query) so any column/view drift surfaces as a translation/mapping exception in the test rather than at report runtime. Mark it `[Trait("Category","RequiresDb")]` so it only runs where the canonical DB is reachable.
verification: Run `dotnet build` in Backend/ to confirm the Program.cs change compiles.
Start the API with Database:MigrateOnStartup=true against a DB missing the latest migration; confirm __EFMigrationsHistory updates and ExcelExportJobs table exists.
Run `pwsh Backend/scaffold-tradenet.ps1 -Verify`: exits 0 when in sync, non-zero after a hand edit to TradeNetDbContext.cs.
Run `dotnet test Backend.Tests`: schema-validation test passes against canonical DB, fails with a mapping/translation error when a mapped column/view is missing.
pitfalls: "Call Migrate() ONLY on ApplicationDbContext; calling Migrate()/EnsureCreated() on TradeNetDbContext (which has no migrations) would throw or try to create tables in an externally-owned DB. Order matters: build the app, then migrate inside a scope before app.Run(). MigrateOnStartup adds a brief startup delay and requires the app's DB principal to have DDL rights — if DBAs apply migrations out-of-band, set MigrateOnStartup=false and keep it as a deploy step instead. The scaffold -Verify check needs the canonical connection string injected via env var (TRADENET_SCAFFOLD_CONN); never commit that string. Re-scaffolding with --force overwrites Model/TradeNet and TradeNetDbContext.cs, so any manual edits to those generated files (e.g. partial OnModelCreatingPartial hooks) must live in a separate non-generated partial file to survive regeneration. The Backend.Tests fixture currently uses EnsureCreatedAsync() against the scaffold to build a throwaway DB — keep the new real-schema validation test separate (Category=RequiresDb) so it doesn't run in environments without the canonical DB."

approach: Introduce one injectable abstraction (IReportRequestValidator) plus an abstract ReportControllerBase to absorb the duplicated TryCreateReportRequest / paging logic, and wrap the static sp_*/ReportQueryService data access behind a thin DI-registered IReportQueryExecutor that takes the (already DI-scoped) TradeNetDbContext, so controllers depend on interfaces and become unit-testable without a real SQL fixture. Replace the one fragile SqlException.Number==2812 string-match fallback in AccountSummaryReportController with a startup proc-existence check cached in the new service.
steps: Read the four cited files plus Backend/Program.cs, Backend/Model/ReportQueryRequest.cs, Backend/Service/ExcelExport/ExcelExportServiceCollectionExtensions.cs (already done). Note the real shared shape: ReportQueryRequest exposes PageIndex/PageSize/SortColumn/SortOrder/FilterColumn/FilterQuery/IncludeTotalCount, and TryCreateReportRequest is duplicated in 157 of 158 controllers (confirmed via `grep -rl TryCreateReportRequest Backend/Controllers | wc -l`). The 2812 string-match fallback exists in exactly ONE file, AccountSummaryReportController.cs:160-164 — do NOT treat it as widespread.

Create Backend/Service/Reports/IReportRequestValidator.cs with interface IReportRequestValidator and sealed class ReportRequestValidator, exposing string? ValidateDateRange(DateTime fromDate, DateTime toDate) that reproduces the exact three checks currently inlined in every TryCreateReportRequest (FromDate==default -> 'FromDate is required.', ToDate==default -> 'ToDate is required.', ToDate<FromDate -> 'ToDate must be greater than or equal to FromDate.'). Keep the messages byte-identical so API responses don't change.

Register it in Backend/Program.cs immediately after line 93 (the ICommonService<> registration, before AddExcelExportQueue): builder.Services.AddSingleton<API.Service.Reports.IReportRequestValidator, API.Service.Reports.ReportRequestValidator>(). Singleton is safe — the validator is stateless.

Create Backend/Controllers/Report/ReportControllerBase.cs: an abstract ControllerBase subclass that takes IReportRequestValidator via its constructor and exposes the shared DefaultPageSize=10 / MaxPageSize=1000 constants and a NormalizePaging(pageIndex, pageSize) helper matching the math already in AccountSummaryReportController.cs:49-52 and sp_HSCodeReport.StoredProcedure.cs:30-31.

Migrate AccountSummaryReportController to the new base as the reference conversion: make it inherit ReportControllerBase, inject IReportRequestValidator through the constructor (alongside the existing TradeNetDbContext and IExcelExportJobService), and rewrite the body of TryCreateReportRequest so the date checks call Validator.ValidateDateRange(...) and only the report-specific mapping to sp_AccountSummaryReportRequest stays local. Replace the inline paging math (lines 49-52) with NormalizePaging.

Introduce a thin data-access abstraction so controllers stop binding to static sp_* methods + concrete TradeNetDbContext. Add Backend/Service/Reports/IReportQueryExecutor.cs (interface) and a ReportQueryExecutor implementation registered AddScoped in Program.cs that takes TradeNetDbContext via DI. Give it (a) the generic CreatePagedResultAsync<T>(IQueryable<T>, ReportQueryRequest) currently living in the static ReportQueryService (move/forward that logic — keep ReportQueryService delegating to it for now to avoid touching all callers at once), and (b) a bool ProcedureExists(string name) that queries sys.objects (e.g. db.Database.SqlQueryRaw<int>("SELECT CASE WHEN OBJECT_ID(@p0,'P') IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END", name)) and caches the result per proc name for the lifetime of the app.

In AccountSummaryReportController, delete the IsMissingPaginationProcedure(SqlException) helper (lines 160-164) and the catch (SqlException ex) when (IsMissingPaginationProcedure(ex)) guard (lines 70-76). Instead, decide the path up-front: if _queryExecutor.ProcedureExists("sp_AccountSummaryReport_pagination") run the paginated proc, else fall back to sp_AccountSummaryReport.Query(...) + the executor's CreatePagedResultAsync. This removes the dependency on SqlException.Number==2812 and Message.Contains, which break under localized SQL Server messages or a proc rename.

Move the inline LINQ in ImportLicenceBySectionReportController.BuildRowsAsync (lines 101-163) out of the controller. Create a static helper (mirror the existing sp_ImportLicenceDetailReport_Fast.GetSectionRowsAsync already called in WriteRowsAsync at line 197, or add a sibling method) that returns the List<ReportAggregateResult>, so the controller no longer holds join+aggregation logic that every other report keeps in StoredProcedureToLinq. Keep the 5-minute IMemoryCache wrapper and the ColumnTotals footer (lines 64-94) in the controller.

Add a unit test in Backend.Tests proving the abstraction pays off: a test that constructs AccountSummaryReportController with a mocked IReportRequestValidator (and a mocked/fake IReportQueryExecutor) and asserts that a request with ToDate < FromDate returns BadRequest('ToDate must be greater than or equal to FromDate.') without any SQL Server — i.e. it does NOT use ReportSqlServerFixture. This is the concrete testability win the finding asks for.

Roll the same base-class + validator + executor conversion across the remaining 156 controllers incrementally (this is the 'Large' part of the effort). Each conversion is mechanical: inherit ReportControllerBase, inject IReportRequestValidator, replace inline date checks with Validator.ValidateDateRange and inline paging with NormalizePaging. Do them in small batches and run `dotnet build` + `dotnet test` between batches.
verification: dotnet build the Backend project succeeds with no errors after each batch: `dotnet build "Backend/Backend.csproj"` (or the solution).

Run the new no-SQL unit test: `dotnet test Backend.Tests --filter FullyQualifiedName~AccountSummary` and confirm the BadRequest validation test passes WITHOUT instantiating ReportSqlServerFixture (proves controllers are now mockable).

Functional parity: POST /api/AccountSummaryReport with a valid body and confirm the JSON (rows, totalCount, ColumnTotals) is byte-for-byte identical to pre-change output; POST with ToDate < FromDate and confirm the response is still 400 'ToDate must be greater than or equal to FromDate.'

Confirm the missing-proc fallback still works: on a DB where sp_AccountSummaryReport_pagination is absent, POST /api/AccountSummaryReport and verify it returns data via the Query() fallback path (now selected by ProcedureExists, not by catching SqlException 2812).
POST /api/ImportLicenceBySectionReport before and after the BuildRowsAsync extraction and diff the response — rows, paging, and the noOfLicences/totalValue ColumnTotals footer must be unchanged.
Run the full existing suite: `dotnet test` — all previously green tests still pass.
pitfalls: Honor the verifier corrections: (a) the SqlException.Number==2812 + Message.Contains fallback lives in ONLY AccountSummaryReportController.cs:160-164 — do not refactor a 'widespread' fallback that doesn't exist; (b) do NOT frame this work as the cause of the per-report parity complaints (those have documented root causes: lookup wiring, missing ColumnTotals, header mismatches) — this is a Low-severity testability/maintainability change only. Keep validation message strings byte-identical or you change the API contract and break frontend error handling. TradeNetDbContext is registered with AddDbContextPool (scoped); IReportQueryExecutor must be AddScoped (not singleton) so it never captures a pooled DbContext beyond a request. The Excel handler path resolves controllers by reflection via ControllerStreamingExcelReportJobHandler / ExcelExportServiceCollectionExtensions (auto-discovers IStreamingExcelReport implementers) — controllers gaining a new constructor parameter (IReportRequestValidator) must still be resolvable from the request-scope DI container, so register the validator before AddExcelExportQueue and verify an Excel export still enqueues. Roll out the 156-controller conversion in small batches behind build+test gates; the base-class change alone is low-risk and independently revertable, but the executor extraction touches data access so land it after the validator/base-class change, not before.
estimatedTime: Validator + base class + AccountSummary conversion + executor/proc-existence + ImportLicenceBySection extraction + first unit test: ~1.5 days. Full rollout across the remaining 156 controllers: ~1 week of mechanical batched work.

approach: Turn the silent `?? [db]` fallback in ReportTestHelper.CreateController into a loud, descriptive failure that names the unsatisfiable constructor parameters, and add a small set of explicit (non-reflection) edge-case tests against one representative controller (BorderExportLicenceNewReportNewReportController) asserting its documented validation/clamping for inverted dates, oversized PageSize, and a metacharacter-laden FilterQuery.
steps: Open Backend.Tests/ReportTestHelper.cs. In CreateController (lines 75-108), the LINQ pipeline builds `object[] constructorArguments` from the longest constructor whose params are ALL in the allowlist (TradeNetDbContext/ICountryCache/IMemoryCache/IExcelExportJobService), then falls back to `?? [db]` on line 94. Replace that `?? [db]` so an unsatisfiable controller becomes a loud xUnit failure instead.

Refactor the expression so the matched constructor parameter list is captured first (it is currently consumed inline). Compute `var satisfiableParameters = controllerType.GetConstructors().OrderByDescending(c => c.GetParameters().Length).Select(c => c.GetParameters()).FirstOrDefault(parameters => parameters.All(IsSupportedDependency));` where IsSupportedDependency is a small local/static predicate holding the four allowlisted types.

When `satisfiableParameters` is null, fail loudly: build a message listing the constructor signatures the harness could not satisfy, e.g. `Assert.Fail($"{controllerType.Name} has no constructor whose parameters are all supported by the test harness. Supported: TradeNetDbContext, ICountryCache, IMemoryCache, IExcelExportJobService. Constructors found: {string.Join(\" | \", controllerType.GetConstructors().Select(c => \"(\" + string.Join(\", \", c.GetParameters().Select(p => p.ParameterType.Name)) + \")\")))}.");`. Keep the existing `parameter.ParameterType switch` projection (lines 86-92) to map the satisfiable parameters to instances (EmptyCountryCache / MemoryCache / FakeExcelExportJobService / db). This makes the previously-untested 'new dependency added' path a named failing test rather than a generic Activator.CreateInstance throw or a wrong-constructor silent pass.

Add a new test file Backend.Tests/ReportControllerEdgeCaseTests.cs targeting BorderExportLicenceNewReportNewReportController directly (it has the documented MaxPageSize=1000 clamp at line 26/51 and the `ToDate < FromDate` BadRequest at lines 137-141). Construct it with `new BorderExportLicenceNewReportNewReportController(db, new ReportTestHelper-provided fake)` — since FakeExcelExportJobService and CreateInMemoryDbContext are internal to the helper, either expose a thin internal factory (e.g. `internal static (TradeNetDbContext db, IExcelExportJobService excel) CreateExcelDeps(string name)`) or reuse CreateController via reflection but cast to the concrete type. Simplest: add `internal static IExcelExportJobService CreateFakeExcelJobService() => new FakeExcelExportJobService();` to ReportTestHelper and `new BorderExportLicenceNewReportNewReportController(db, ReportTestHelper.CreateFakeExcelJobService())` in the test.

In ReportControllerEdgeCaseTests, write `Post_with_inverted_date_range_returns_BadRequest`: set `FromDate = new DateTime(2024,1,1)`, `ToDate = new DateTime(2023,1,1)`, call `await controller.Post(request)`, and assert the result is BadRequest with message 'ToDate must be greater than or equal to FromDate.' (the controller returns `ActionResult<ApiResult<sp_NewReportResult>>`, so inspect `.Result` as `BadRequestObjectResult`).

Write `Post_clamps_oversized_PageSize_to_MaxPageSize`: this needs a queryable backing — since the in-memory provider cannot run the raw-SQL `sp_NewReport.ExecuteAsync`, assert the clamp at the unit level instead by exposing/observing the computed pageSize, OR keep it deterministic by asserting on `ApiResult` paging metadata when run against the SQL fixture (mirror ReportEndpointSmokeTests' IClassFixture<ReportSqlServerFixture> pattern). If SQL is unavailable in CI, instead add an assertion test that sets `PageSize = 5000` and verifies, via the SQL fixture path used by ReportEndpointSmokeTests, that the returned `PageSize` in ApiResult is 1000 (Math.Min(request.PageSize, MaxPageSize)).

Write `Post_with_filter_metacharacters_does_not_throw`: set `FilterColumn`/`FilterQuery` to an adversarial string such as `"');DROP TABLE x;--"` plus LINQ-dynamic metacharacters like `"1 == 1 || \"\".Length"`, valid FromDate/ToDate, run via the SQL fixture, and assert the call returns a non-null ApiResult (no unhandled exception, no SQL leak) — documenting that FilterQuery is parameterized/sanitized downstream.

Run `dotnet test Backend.Tests` and confirm all existing reflection-driven theories still pass (the allowlist covers 100% of current controllers, so the new throw path must NOT trigger today) and the three new edge-case tests pass.
verification: Run `dotnet test Backend.Tests` from the repo root: all existing reflection theories (ReportEndpointSmokeTests, ReportControllerBranchDefaultsTests, etc.) still pass — confirming the new Assert.Fail path is NOT hit for any current controller (the allowlist covers 100% today).
The new ReportControllerEdgeCaseTests.Post_with_inverted_date_range_returns_BadRequest passes (asserts BadRequest + exact message from BorderExportLicenceNewReportNewReportController lines 137-141).

Regression check for the loud-failure behavior: temporarily add a throwaway controller in Backend with a constructor taking an unsupported dependency (e.g. ILogger<T>), run the smoke theory, and confirm it now fails with the descriptive 'has no constructor the test harness can satisfy ... Constructors found: (ILogger`1)' message rather than a generic Activator.CreateInstance error or a silent pass. Remove the throwaway controller after.

PageSize/FilterQuery edge tests (if wired to ReportSqlServerFixture) pass when the localdb/SQL fixture is available; PageSize=5000 yields ApiResult.PageSize==1000 and the metacharacter FilterQuery returns a non-null ApiResult without an unhandled exception.
pitfalls: Ordering: capture `satisfiableParameters` (the ParameterInfo[]) BEFORE projecting to instances — the original code consumed the params inline so the null-check and the descriptive message could not see them. The `!` after the null-check is safe because Assert.Fail throws.; ReportTestHelper is a `static` class and EmptyCountryCache/FakeExcelExportJobService are PRIVATE nested classes. To let ReportControllerEdgeCaseTests build a real controller, expose `internal static IExcelExportJobService CreateFakeExcelJobService()` rather than making the nested class public.; The PageSize=1000 clamp and FilterQuery behavior run the raw-SQL stored proc `sp_NewReport.ExecuteAsync`, which the EF Core in-memory provider cannot execute. Use the SQL fixture path (IClassFixture<ReportSqlServerFixture>, mirroring ReportEndpointSmokeTests) for those two assertions, or scope them to skip when no SQL server is reachable; the inverted-date-range test stays in-memory because validation returns BadRequest before any DB call.; Do not lower the harness allowlist or relax the predicate to make it 'pass' — the whole point is that a new unsupported dependency should fail loudly so a maintainer adds a fake.; Rollback is trivial and isolated to Backend.Tests (no runtime/production code changes); the only product-code touch would be the throwaway controller used for the regression check, which must be deleted.

approach: Add a single new xUnit test file to the existing Backend.Tests project that pins down two pure/deterministic behaviors that are currently untested: ExcelExportHasher.ComputeHash canonicalization (grid-only fields ignored, real filters change the hash, key order is canonical) and ExcelExportFileStore path handling (Exists/OpenRead/Delete over a temp dir, plus the traversal guard). No production code changes — this is pure coverage to lock in correct current behavior.
steps: Create a new test file Backend.Tests/ExcelExportHasherAndFileStoreTests.cs. The project already references xUnit and the API project, and has a global `using Xunit;` (Backend.Tests.csproj ItemGroup), so no new packages are needed. Use namespace `Backend.Tests` to match StreamingExcelWriterTests.cs.

Add `using API.Service.ExcelExport;` (for ExcelExportHasher, ExcelExportFileStore, ExcelExportOptions) and `using Microsoft.Extensions.Options;` plus `using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting;` and `using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http;` for the IWebHostEnvironment stub.

ExcelExportHasher tests — write raw JSON strings (ComputeHash takes a requestJson string, per ExcelExportHasher.cs:29). Test 1 `Ignores_grid_only_fields`: hash a JSON with only fromDate/type, then hash the same JSON with the seven IgnoredFields (pageIndex, pageSize, sortColumn, sortOrder, filterColumn, filterQuery, includeTotalCount) added — these names match ReportQueryRequest's grid fields exactly — and Assert.Equal the two hashes for the same reportKey.

Test 2 `Real_filter_changes_hash`: two requests differing only in a genuine filter field (e.g. fromDate `2026-01-01` vs `2026-02-01`, and a second case differing on `type`) must produce different hashes via Assert.NotEqual. Also assert a different reportKey with identical JSON yields a different hash (reportKey is prefixed into the payload at ExcelExportHasher.cs:32).

Test 3 `Canonical_key_order_is_stable`: two JSON objects with the same fields in different orders and different key casing (e.g. `{"Type":"X","fromDate":"2026-01-01"}` vs `{"fromdate":"2026-01-01","type":"X"}`) hash identically, exercising the lower-case + OrderBy in Canonicalize (ExcelExportHasher.cs:55-59). Also assert ComputeHash with empty/whitespace requestJson does not throw and is stable (the `IsNullOrWhiteSpace` short-circuit returns string.Empty).

ExcelExportFileStore tests — construct the store with an ABSOLUTE temp StorageRoot so Path.IsPathRooted(configured) is true and env.ContentRootPath is never read (ExcelExportFileStore.cs:33-36). Build options via `Options.Create(new ExcelExportOptions { StorageRoot = Path.Combine(Path.GetTempPath(), "excelexport-tests-" + Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N")) })`. Supply a minimal IWebHostEnvironment stub (a small private nested class returning empty/temp values) since the ctor signature requires it even though the rooted branch won't dereference ContentRootPath.

Test 4 `Write_then_read_roundtrips_and_exists_reports_true`: call BuildRelativePath("r.xlsx"), OpenWrite(rel) and write bytes, then Assert Exists(rel) is true, GetSize(rel) equals the byte count, and OpenRead(rel) returns the same bytes.

Test 5 `Exists_false_and_delete_is_safe_for_missing`: Assert Exists(null) and Exists("") are false, Exists("nope/missing.xlsx") is false, and Delete(null)/Delete("missing.xlsx") do not throw (Delete returns early on null/empty and guards on File.Exists, ExcelExportFileStore.cs:66-78). Then Delete an existing file and assert Exists becomes false.

Test 6 `Traversal_path_is_rejected`: per the verifier note, assert the EXISTING guard rather than implying it is missing — `Assert.Throws<InvalidOperationException>(() => store.OpenRead(Path.Combine("..", "..", "escape.xlsx")))`; the FullPath check at ExcelExportFileStore.cs:80-89 throws "Resolved export path escapes the storage root." Use IDisposable on the test class (or a try/finally) to delete the temp StorageRoot after each test.
Build and run: from the repo root run `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj`. All new tests plus existing ones should pass.
verification: From repo root: `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj` — all six new tests pass alongside the existing suite (StreamingExcelWriterTests etc.).
Run only the new tests: `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ExcelExportHasherAndFileStoreTests`.

Regression-guard check: temporarily add a fake field to ExcelExportHasher.IgnoredFields (e.g. "fromDate") and re-run — Real_filter_changes_hash should fail, proving the test actually catches a broken ignore set; then revert.
Confirm no leftover temp dirs: after the run, the per-test directories under the OS temp path (excelexport-tests-*) are removed by Dispose().
pitfalls: "Keep StorageRoot ABSOLUTE in the test (Path.GetTempPath()-based). If you pass a relative path, ExcelExportFileStore takes the env.ContentRootPath branch (ExcelExportFileStore.cs:34-36) and the FakeEnv's ContentRootPath would govern where files land. No Moq package is referenced in Backend.Tests.csproj, so do NOT add Mock<IWebHostEnvironment> — use the hand-written FakeEnv nested class as shown (or you'd have to add the Moq PackageReference). The class implements IDisposable so xUnit tears down the temp dir per test; do not make _root static/shared across tests since BuildRelativePath shards by UTC day and parallel tests could collide. This is additive test-only code — no production behavior changes and nothing to roll back beyond deleting the file."

approach: Add one reflection-driven xUnit Theory to ReportQueryTranslationTests that iterates every report controller via the existing ReportTestHelper.ControllerCases() harness, builds each report's query with the existing ReportTestHelper.CreateQuery(...) (which already locates the Query(db,request) method), and asserts ToQueryString() succeeds — turning DB-free EF translation coverage from a hand-picked dozen into every Query-backed report. The new theory must tolerate the _Fast-only detail/summary controllers (ImportLicenceDetail, ExportPermitDetail, Border detail, Alcoholic*, etc.) that expose no public static Query method, since those go through CreatePagedResultAsync/CreateSectionPagedResultAsync and are a separately-tracked gap.
steps: Open Backend.Tests/ReportQueryTranslationTests.cs. Keep all four existing tests (Hs_code_empty_branch_can_still_translate_dynamic_sorting, Empty_switch_branch_can_still_translate_dynamic_sorting, Aggregate_report_form_type_branch_translates_to_sql, Pending_report_translates_to_sql) — they pin the empty/'Unknown' FormType switch-default branches that a neutral reflection request would NOT exercise, so they are complementary, not redundant.

Add a MemberData source method `public static IEnumerable<object[]> AllReportControllers() => ReportTestHelper.ControllerCases();`. ControllerCases() (ReportTestHelper.cs:33) already reflects over every non-abstract *Controller in the Backend.Controllers.Report namespace — this is the same source ReportEndpointSmokeTests and ReportSeededDatabaseSmokeTests use, so coverage tracks the controller set automatically.

Add the new `[Theory]` `Report_controller_query_translates_to_sql(Type controllerType)` that calls `ReportTestHelper.CreateQuery(controllerType, db)` against `ReportTestHelper.CreateSqlServerDbContext()` and asserts `query.Skip(0).Take(10).ToQueryString()` is non-empty. ToQueryString() is the same compile-only, connectionless translation check already used at ReportQueryTranslationTests.cs:26/41/93/110 — no DB connection is opened, so it stays CI-safe.

Add the `FastOnlyControllersWithoutQueryMethod` HashSet and the early `return` guard exactly as in the patch. CreateQuery (ReportTestHelper.cs:152-168) throws InvalidOperationException '... is missing the expected Query method' for controllers whose Post() is backed only by sp_*_Fast.CreatePagedResultAsync / CreateSectionPagedResultAsync (these expose 0 `.Query(` calls). Without this guard the theory would fail for the detail/summary/voucher *_Fast controllers. The set is: ImportLicence/ExportLicence/ImportPermit/ExportPermit detail, the four Border detail controllers + BorderImportLicenceDetailReportPending, and the three AlcoholicBeveragesImportation* controllers.

Build and run only this class to confirm it goes green and the case count jumped from ~12 to ~60+: from the repo root run `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ReportQueryTranslationTests"`. (Quote the path — the repo path contains spaces.)

If any non-_Fast controller now fails translation, that is a real EF-translation regression in its StoredProcedureToLinq/sp_*.cs Query method — fix the LINQ there (do not add it to the skip set). Only genuinely Query-less _Fast controllers belong in FastOnlyControllersWithoutQueryMethod.

Optional but recommended to satisfy the verifier's note about the separate _Fast gap: add a follow-up TODO or a small xUnit [Fact] asserting `FastOnlyControllersWithoutQueryMethod` matches the set of controllers calling CreatePagedResultAsync/CreateSectionPagedResultAsync, so a future *_Fast translation test (or removal of a _Fast path) keeps the skip list honest.
verification: From the repo root: `dotnet test Backend.Tests/Backend.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ReportQueryTranslationTests"` — expect all green, with the new Report_controller_query_translates_to_sql theory contributing one case per report controller (roughly 60+, up from the prior ~12 total).

Temporarily break a single previously-uncovered query (e.g. add `.Where(x => x.SomeString.Normalize() == "x")` to a StoredProcedureToLinq/sp_*.cs Query method that EF cannot translate), re-run the filter, and confirm Report_controller_query_translates_to_sql for that controller now FAILS at ToQueryString() — proving the new theory actually exercises that query. Revert the change afterward.

Confirm CI safety: the run must not require or open a real SQL Server connection — CreateSqlServerDbContext targets (localdb) only to build the SqlServer provider model for ToQueryString(); no .ToList()/EnsureCreated is called in this theory.
pitfalls: "Do NOT delete the four existing curated tests — they cover the empty/'Unknown' FormType switch-default branches that the neutral reflection request will not hit. Accuracy on scope: this approach covers the ~68 Query(db,request)-backed reports (the audit's '93 files' is loose — the StoredProcedureToLinq dir also includes _old / search / .StoredProcedure helper files and the GetRequest* helpers). The _Fast detail/summary/voucher paths (CreatePagedResultAsync / CreateSectionPagedResultAsync) remain UNCOVERED by this theory by design — calling those out is the verifier-confirmed separate gap, so don't claim 'all 93' in the PR description. Ordering: the skip list keys off controllerType.Name (e.g. 'ImportLicenceDetailReportController'); if a controller is renamed or a _Fast report later gains a public Query method, update FastOnlyControllersWithoutQueryMethod accordingly or the theory will start (correctly) failing/throwing. Rollback is trivial — the change is additive to one test file with no production-code or schema impact."

approach: Stop deriving auth/role from the mutable `userid`/`permission` localStorage keys. Instead decode the stored JWT, verify its `exp`, and derive `isAuthenticated`, `id`, and `permission` from the token's verified claims (the backend already enforces `[Authorize]` on every data action, so the client guard is UX-only). Expired/malformed tokens are cleared and treated as logged-out.
steps: Open Frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx. Add the `decodeJwt` and `userFromToken` helpers shown in the patch (above the AuthProvider component). They base64url-decode the JWT payload, reject if `exp` is missing or in the past (Date.now() >= exp*1000), and read id from `unique_name`/`nameid` and role from `role` — these are the standard names JWTManagerService.cs emits for ClaimTypes.Name and ClaimTypes.Role.

Replace the `user` useState initializer (lines 22-30) so it no longer reads `localStorage.getItem('userid')` / `localStorage.getItem('permission')`. Initialize `user` via `userFromToken(localStorage.getItem('token'))` so identity and role come only from the verified token.

Reorder so the `token` useState (currently lines 32-34) is declared first and, in its initializer, call `userFromToken` on the stored token; if it returns null, remove the `token`, `userid`, and `permission` keys from localStorage and initialize token to null. This clears spoofed/expired state on app load.

In `login` (lines 36-65), after `setToken(temp.token)`, compute `const derived = userFromToken(temp.token);` — if it is null, `return false`; otherwise `setUser(derived); return true;`. Keep the existing `localStorage.setItem('token', temp.token)` line. The `userid`/`permission` setItem lines can stay for backward compat but are no longer the gating source; optionally remove them.
Leave `isAuthenticated: !!user` on line 77 as-is — it is now backed by the verified-token-derived `user`, so it flips to false on expiry/tamper.
Leave Frontend/src/routes/ProtectedRoute.tsx unchanged: its `if (!auth?.isAuthenticated)` redirect (line 8) now correctly reflects token validity. Treat this guard as UX-only.

Confirm (no change needed) that the backend continues to enforce real authZ: Backend/Controllers/BaseAPIController.cs declares `[AllowAnonymous]` at class level (line 15) but every action overrides with `[Authorize]` (lines 28, 50, 65, 90, 100, 113), and AuthController.Login is correctly `[AllowAnonymous]` (line 23). Do NOT add `[AllowAnonymous]` at action level on any report/data controller. Grep to be safe: `grep -rn "AllowAnonymous" Backend/Controllers` should show it only at BaseAPIController class level and on AuthController.Login.

Optional hardening (recommended, separate small change): in Frontend/src/services/AxiosInstance.ts add a response interceptor that, on a 401, clears the token/userid/permission keys and redirects to /auth/signin, so a server-rejected (expired) token also tears down client state.
verification: Build/lint the frontend: from Frontend/ run `npm run build` (or `npx tsc --noEmit`) — no type errors from the new helpers.

Spoof test (must now FAIL to grant access): open devtools on a logged-out session, run `localStorage.setItem('userid','x'); localStorage.setItem('permission','Admin');` (do NOT set a token), reload — ProtectedRoute should redirect to /auth/signin because `user` is derived from the absent/invalid token, not from those keys.
Tamper test: set `localStorage.setItem('token','not.a.jwt')` and reload — `userFromToken` returns null, keys are cleared, and you are redirected to sign-in.
Expiry test: log in normally, then in devtools paste a token whose `exp` is in the past (or temporarily change the check to `exp*1000 - 60_000`) — reload should treat you as logged out.
Happy path: log in with valid creds; `atob(localStorage.getItem('token').split('.')[1])` shows `unique_name`/`role`/`exp`; protected routes render and the user object reflects the token's role.
Backend confirmation: with a tampered/absent Authorization header, hit any report endpoint (e.g. via curl) and confirm it returns 401 — proves the client guard bypass yields no data.
pitfalls: This is a UX-only guard, not a security boundary. The actual protection is the backend [Authorize] on BaseAPIController actions — verifier confirmed it is in place. Do not relax those, and do not introduce action-level [AllowAnonymous] on data/report controllers.; Claim name mapping: JWTManagerService.cs uses ClaimTypes.Name and ClaimTypes.Role, which serialize to `unique_name` and `role` in the raw JWT (System.IdentityModel default), NOT the long URI claim types. The helper reads `unique_name`/`nameid` and `role`; if a future token uses URI-style claim keys, the decode will yield null and log the user out — keep the fallback fields in sync if claim emission changes.; Order matters in AuthProvider: declare/initialize `token` state before `user` (or compute both from the same `localStorage.getItem('token')`), otherwise the user initializer may read a token the component hasn't validated/cleared yet.; Do not perform any client-side cryptographic signature verification — the HMAC key (JWT:Key) must never ship to the browser. We only decode and check `exp`; signature trust stays server-side.; Rollback is trivial: revert AuthContext.tsx to deriving user from `userid`/`permission`. No data migration and no backend changes are required, so this can ship independently.; exp is seconds since epoch in JWT; multiply by 1000 before comparing with Date.now() (already handled in the patch). A missing exp must be treated as invalid, not as 'never expires'.

approach: Move the authenticated Timeline route into the existing `<ProtectedRoute/>` children block in `Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx`, and delete the leftover `/Test` debug route (plus its now-unused import) from the production router. Leave the static `Certificate` route public after confirming it holds no sensitive data. Note this is client-side route gating only — the real authorization boundary is backend API JWT auth, which this finding does not change.
steps: Open Frontend/src/routes/routes.tsx.

Delete the standalone /Timeline route object currently at lines 200-214 (the block `{ path: '/Timeline', element: (<PageWrapper><DashboardLayout /></PageWrapper>), errorElement: <ErrorPage />, children: [{ path: 'Detail', element: <TimelinePage /> }] }`).

Re-add that same /Timeline route object as a new entry inside the `children` array of the `<ProtectedRoute/>` element (the array opened at line 111, after the existing `/Report` entry that ends at line 170) so it renders only for authenticated users — same shape as the /User and /Report entries.

Delete the entire /Test route object at lines 215-233 (the `{ path: '/Test', ... children: [{ path: 'New', element: <Test /> }, { path: 'Edit/:id', element: <Test /> }] }` block) — it is a debug page and should not ship to production.
Remove the now-unused import `import Test from '../pages/Test.tsx';` at line 23 (TypeScript/Vite build will flag it as unused otherwise).

Decide what to do with the dead navigation calls in src/components/Chat/ChatList.tsx:26 and src/components/Chat/ChatListMobile.tsx:26, which navigate to `/Test/Edit/...`. Since these are only reachable through the Test debug page being removed, the cleanest option is to leave the Chat components as-is (they become unreachable) OR, if you want full cleanup, remove the <Chat/> debug usage too — but at minimum do NOT rely on /Test existing. No production menu links to /Test, so removing the route does not break user-facing navigation.

Confirm Certificate (Frontend/src/pages/certificate/Certificate.tsx) is intentionally public and contains only static placeholder markup with no token-bearing data fetch; if it ever gains a data fetch, move its two route entries (lines 100-107) under <ProtectedRoute/> as well. As of now it is fine to leave public.
verification: Build the frontend to confirm no unused-import / type errors after removing the Test import: from Frontend/ run `npm run build` (Vite + tsc) — it must succeed.

In a logged-OUT browser session (clear auth token / localStorage), navigate to /Timeline/Detail — you should be redirected to /auth/signin by ProtectedRoute instead of rendering the DashboardLayout shell.
While logged IN, navigate to /Timeline/Detail and confirm the TimelinePage still renders inside DashboardLayout (no regression for authenticated users).
Navigate to /Test/New and /Test/Edit/anything — both should now hit the router's errorElement / 404 (route no longer exists), confirming the debug page is gone from production.
Confirm /Certificate and /Certificate/:id still load (intentionally public, static placeholder).
grep the build output / src to confirm `pages/Test.tsx` is no longer imported by the router: `grep -rn "pages/Test" Frontend/src` should return nothing.
pitfalls: Removing the `Test` import while the /Test route block still references `<Test />` will cause a compile error — delete the route block and the import together. The /Test/Edit navigation calls in ChatList.tsx and ChatListMobile.tsx become dead links once /Test is removed; they are only reachable from the Test debug page itself, so no production flow breaks, but do not re-add /Test to satisfy them. Remember this is purely client-side gating: ProtectedRoute only checks AuthContext.isAuthenticated and can be bypassed by a determined user — it is defense-in-depth, not the security boundary. The actual protection of report/user data must remain enforced by backend JWT [Authorize] auth on the API, which this change does not (and should not) alter. Rollback is trivial: git revert the single-file change to routes.tsx.

approach: Remove the hardcoded localhost and live UAT government host fallbacks from Frontend/src/config.ts; instead read the three VITE_* vars and fail fast (throw at module load) when any required one is missing, so a misbuilt bundle can never silently point at localhost or the UAT backend. Ship a committed .env.example documenting the required keys.
steps: Open Frontend/src/config.ts. Today it defines envConfig with the `??` fallbacks: baseUrl -> 'https://localhost:8000/api/', imageUrl -> 'https://localhost:8000/Image/', qrUrl -> 'https://uatapi.ecomreg.gov.mm/QR/' (lines 2-4). Replace the whole file with a fail-fast reader (see patch). Define a small `requireEnv(name)` helper that reads import.meta.env[name], throws an Error if it is undefined/empty, and returns the value. Build envConfig from requireEnv('VITE_BASE_URL'), requireEnv('VITE_IMAGE_URL'), requireEnv('VITE_QR_URL'). This removes the live UAT hostname (https://uatapi.ecomreg.gov.mm/QR/) from the shipped bundle entirely.

Create Frontend/.env.example (committed) listing the three keys with placeholder/relative values, e.g. VITE_BASE_URL=, VITE_IMAGE_URL=, VITE_QR_URL= with a comment per environment. The repo root .gitignore already ignores `.env` and `.env.*` while allowing `!.env.example`, so the example is committed but real env files are not — no .gitignore change needed.

Ensure each build environment actually supplies the vars. Add a Frontend/.env (local dev, untracked) and the CI/deploy pipeline env (UAT/prod) with the correct VITE_BASE_URL, VITE_IMAGE_URL, VITE_QR_URL. Because the app now throws at startup if any is missing, confirm the deploy job sets all three before running `npm run build` (vite inlines import.meta.env at build time, so they must be present in the build environment, not the runtime container).

Confirm consumers still compile: envConfig.baseUrl is used in src/services/AxiosInstance.ts and src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx; envConfig.imageUrl in src/hooks/useFileUpload.tsx, src/hooks/useFileUploadWithoutCrop.tsx, src/components/Chat/ChatBox.tsx. The exported shape is unchanged (same three string keys), so no consumer edits are required. Note: qrUrl has no consumer outside config.ts (verifier-confirmed) — keep it in the config object for shape parity but it could also be dropped; keeping it is lower risk.
verification: Build with all three vars set (e.g. create Frontend/.env from .env.example) and run `npm run build` in Frontend — it succeeds and `grep -r 'uatapi.ecomreg.gov.mm' Frontend/dist` returns nothing, confirming the UAT host is no longer baked into the bundle.

Build with a var deliberately unset (e.g. `unset VITE_QR_URL` then `npm run dev`/`npm run build`) — the app throws 'Missing required build-time env var VITE_QR_URL' at module load instead of silently falling back.
Run `grep -rn 'localhost:8000\|uatapi.ecomreg.gov.mm' Frontend/src` — returns no matches, proving the hardcoded fallbacks are gone.
Smoke test the running app with valid env: login (uses envConfig.baseUrl via src/services/AxiosInstance.ts) and an image/upload view (envConfig.imageUrl) both resolve against the configured host.
pitfalls: Vite inlines import.meta.env.VITE_* at build time, not runtime — the vars must exist in the CI/build environment that runs `npm run build`, not just in the deployed container, or the new throw will block the build/load. Verify the deploy pipeline before merging.; Fail-fast changes behavior: any environment currently relying on the silent localhost/UAT fallbacks will now error instead of running degraded. Before deploying, ensure every environment (local dev, UAT, prod) has all three VITE_* set.; Do NOT commit a real Frontend/.env — only .env.example. The root .gitignore already covers this; double-check `git status` shows .env untracked.; Rollback: revert config.ts to the prior `??`-fallback version (kept in git history) if a build environment cannot be fixed in time; the .env.example file is harmless to leave in place.

approach: Delete the unused Design Sparx template imagery from Frontend/public/ (showcase, grid/landing-frame demo art, the 188 KB favicon, and the dead HomePage assets) to slim the repo and build output. This is pure dead-asset removal — there is no live page that downloads these, so no compression/optimization work is needed.
steps: Confirm the keep-set first so you don't over-delete. These public assets ARE referenced and must stay: public/moc-logo.png (index.html favicon + src/components/Logo/Logo.tsx, SignIn.tsx, HowToUse.tsx), public/tradenet-logo.png (Logo component), public/get-started.png (src/components/dashboard/default/GetStartedCard/GetStartedCard.tsx), plus public/ship.svg, public/vite.svg, public/firebase-messaging-sw.js, public/_redirects, and public/mocks/.

Delete the clearly-dead heavy assets from Frontend/public/. Run from the Frontend dir: git rm public/favicon.ico public/grid-3d.jpg && git rm -r public/showcase. favicon.ico (188 KB) has zero code references — index.html line 5 and line 31 both point at /moc-logo.png, not favicon.ico. grid-3d.jpg (1.5 MB) appears only inside a commented-out backgroundImage in src/pages/Home.tsx:20. public/showcase/ (4.1 MB) has zero references anywhere in src/ or index.html.

Delete the HomePage-only demo assets: git rm public/grid.jpg public/landing-frame.png. The only consumer is src/pages/HomePage (src/pages/Home.tsx line 96 uses /landing-frame.png; grid.jpg is unused entirely). HomePage is exported from src/pages/index.ts but is NEVER mounted in src/routes/routes.tsx — root path '/' redirects to /auth/signin (routes.tsx line 66) — so these are dead in the shipped app.

Decide on the Storybook-only template doc assets: public/landing-frame.jpg (368 KB), public/me.jpg (8 KB), and public/logo-no-background.png (76 KB) are referenced only by the Design Sparx template doc src/stories/Configure.mdx (imports on lines 3-4; me.jpg via the .mdx text). If you are also removing the leftover template Storybook doc, delete that page and its assets together: git rm src/stories/Configure.mdx public/landing-frame.jpg public/me.jpg public/logo-no-background.png. If you want to keep Storybook's Configure page, leave landing-frame.jpg and logo-no-background.png (and delete only me.jpg, which Configure.mdx no longer imports). Pick one and be consistent.

Clean up the now-dangling dead references so future greps stay accurate: in src/pages/Home.tsx remove the commented-out backgroundImage line 20 that points at /grid-3d.jpg. Optionally delete the unrouted src/pages/Home.tsx + its export in src/pages/index.ts entirely if you confirm HomePage is genuinely unused (it is not in routes.tsx).

Run the production build to confirm nothing 404s or fails to resolve: npm run build (and, if you kept Storybook's Configure.mdx, npm run build-storybook). Vite copies public/ verbatim, so a clean build with no missing-asset errors confirms the deletions are safe.
Commit on a feature branch (not main) with a message describing template-asset cleanup; end the commit body with the required Co-Authored-By trailer.
patch:
# Run from Frontend/ (mind the spaces in the repo path)
cd "/Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Frontend"

# --- Definitely dead (no live references) — ~5.8 MB ---
git rm public/favicon.ico        # 188 KB, index.html uses /moc-logo.png instead
git rm public/grid-3d.jpg        # 1.5 MB, only in a commented line in Home.tsx
git rm -r public/showcase        # 4.1 MB, zero references in src/ or index.html

# --- Dead in the shipped app (HomePage is exported but never routed) — ~0.85 MB ---
git rm public/grid.jpg           # 452 KB, unused
git rm public/landing-frame.png  # 400 KB, only used by unrouted Home.tsx

# --- Storybook template-doc-only assets (delete WITH the leftover doc) ---
git rm src/stories/Configure.mdx public/landing-frame.jpg public/me.jpg public/logo-no-background.png
#   (If keeping Storybook's Configure page, skip this line and instead remove only public/me.jpg)
verification: After deletion, `du -sh public` should drop from ~11 MB to ~4 MB (mostly public/mocks/ remains).
`grep -rn "grid-3d\|showcase\|favicon.ico\|grid.jpg\|landing-frame\|/me.jpg\|logo-no-background" src index.html` returns no LIVE references (only deleted/commented lines are gone).
`npm run build` completes with no 'failed to resolve' / missing-asset errors; the dist/ folder no longer contains showcase/, grid-3d.jpg, favicon.ico, grid.jpg, or landing-frame.*.
Load the running app (npm run preview) — the tab favicon (moc-logo.png), sign-in logo, and GetStartedCard image all still render correctly.
Confirm the favicon network request in DevTools is now /moc-logo.png (~76 KB) and there is no request for the old 188 KB /favicon.ico.
pitfalls: Do NOT delete moc-logo.png, tradenet-logo.png, get-started.png, ship.svg, vite.svg, firebase-messaging-sw.js, _redirects, or the mocks/ folder — these are all live. The single ambiguity is the Storybook Configure.mdx trio (landing-frame.jpg, logo-no-background.png, me.jpg): they are only consumed by that template doc page, so delete the doc and assets together, or keep both — don't delete the assets while leaving Configure.mdx, which would break `npm run build-storybook`. logo-no-background.png is referenced ONLY by Configure.mdx (it is not the app logo — that's moc-logo.png), so it is safe to remove alongside the doc. Use `git rm` (not plain rm) so the files are removed from version control, which is the actual point of this finding (slim repo + build), not on-disk space alone. Rollback is trivial: `git checkout HEAD~1 -- public/ src/stories/Configure.mdx`.

approach: Remove the unused `moment` dependency from `Frontend/package.json` (and lockfile), standardizing all date handling on the already-pervasive `dayjs`, then add an ESLint `no-restricted-imports` ban so any future `import 'moment'` fails lint and never reintroduces the ~70 KB regression.
steps: Confirm there are zero real usages: from /Users/saobranaung/Code/Ministry of Commerce/tradenet-admin-report/tradenet-admin-report/Frontend run `grep -rnE "['\"]moment['\"]" src` — it returns nothing (verified). `react-calendar-timeline` is also unused in src (only the `@types/react-calendar-timeline` devDependency exists, which is type-only and unrelated to runtime moment).
Remove the `moment` dependency line from Frontend/package.json. Delete line 32: `"moment": "^2.30.1",` from the `dependencies` block (keep `dayjs` on line 28).

Update the lockfile and node_modules in one step: from the Frontend directory run `npm uninstall moment`. This rewrites package-lock.json and prunes node_modules. (Equivalent to editing package.json then `npm install`.)

Add an ESLint ban so a future `import ... from 'moment'` is caught. Edit Frontend/.eslintrc.cjs and add a `no-restricted-imports` rule under `rules`. The project uses the legacy `.eslintrc.cjs` (no flat `eslint.config.js` exists), so add it there.
Run the lint script to confirm config validity: from Frontend run `npm run lint`. It must complete without erroring on the new rule (`--max-warnings 0` is already configured in package.json line 11).

Optional but recommended for the supply-chain/CVE concern: also verify nothing transitively requires the top-level moment by checking the build still produces a bundle of the same size — run `npm run build` and confirm it succeeds (rc-picker/antd treat moment as optional and are wired to dayjs, so no failures expected).
verification: `grep -rnE "['\"]moment['\"]" /Users/.../Frontend/src` returns no results (it already does).
`grep -n '"moment"' Frontend/package.json` returns nothing after the change; `grep moment Frontend/package-lock.json` shows no top-level moment entry.

`npm run lint` in Frontend passes; then create a throwaway file with `import moment from 'moment';` and re-run lint — it must fail with the no-restricted-imports error message, then delete the throwaway file.
`npm run build` in Frontend succeeds and the app/Storybook still build (antd date pickers and the dayjs-using report pages render dates unchanged).
pitfalls: Do NOT edit package.json by hand and skip the lockfile — run `npm uninstall moment` (npm is the package manager here; package-lock.json present, no yarn/pnpm lock) so package-lock.json and node_modules stay in sync, otherwise CI `npm ci` may fail.; Keep `@types/react-calendar-timeline` and `dayjs` intact — react-calendar-timeline is not imported in src and its types are type-only, so they are unrelated to this change.; The ban targets the legacy `.eslintrc.cjs`. If the repo later migrates to ESLint flat config (`eslint.config.js`), the `no-restricted-imports` rule must be ported there too or the guard silently disappears.; Rollback is trivial: `npm install moment@^2.30.1` and revert the two file edits.

approach: All 6 files using `import * as _ from 'lodash'` are unrouted starter-template demo code. Since none are reachable from `src/routes/routes.tsx`, the cleanest fix is to delete the dead files (and their barrel exports / stories); if any must be kept, replace the namespace import with per-method named imports so the import is tree-shakeable.
steps: Confirm the files are dead code: `src/routes/routes.tsx` imports none of these. The cited 3 are `src/components/dashboard/learning/StudyStatisticsCard/StudyStatisticsCard.tsx:4`, `src/pages/corporate/Faqs.tsx:3`, `src/pages/userAccount/Help.tsx:11`. A repo-wide grep (`grep -rn "from 'lodash'" Frontend/src`) shows 3 MORE files with the same pattern that the finding undercounted: `src/components/dashboard/default/EarningsCard/EarningsCard.tsx:4`, `src/components/dashboard/marketing/CampaignsAdsCard/CampaignsAdsCard.tsx:21`, `src/components/dashboard/logistics/DeliveryAnalyticsCard/DeliveryAnalyticsCard.tsx:5`. Fix all 6.

PREFERRED (delete dead code). Remove the page files: `Frontend/src/pages/corporate/Faqs.tsx` and `Frontend/src/pages/userAccount/Help.tsx`. Then remove their barrel re-exports: in `Frontend/src/pages/corporate/index.ts` delete the line `export { CorporateFaqPage } from './Faqs.tsx';`, and in `Frontend/src/pages/userAccount/index.ts` delete `export { UserProfileHelpPage } from './Help.tsx';`.

Remove the dashboard demo card directories: `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/learning/StudyStatisticsCard/` (also delete its sibling `StudyStatisticssCard.stories.tsx` in that folder), `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/default/EarningsCard/`, `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/marketing/CampaignsAdsCard/`, and `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/logistics/DeliveryAnalyticsCard/`. Then delete the matching re-export lines in the dashboard barrels: `StudyStatisticsCard` from `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/learning/index.ts` (and `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/index.ts:43`), `EarningsCard` from `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/default/index.ts`, `CampaignsAdsCard` from `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/marketing/index.ts`, `DeliveryAnalyticsCard` from `Frontend/src/components/dashboard/logistics/index.ts`. Search each barrel: `grep -rn "StudyStatisticsCard\|EarningsCard\|CampaignsAdsCard\|DeliveryAnalyticsCard" Frontend/src/components/dashboard` and strip every export line referencing the deleted components.

ALTERNATIVE (if any file must be kept rather than deleted): replace the namespace import with named imports of only the methods used. StudyStatisticsCard uses `_.sortBy`; Faqs uses `_.chain/.groupBy/.map/.orderBy`; Help uses `_.chain/.orderBy/.uniqBy/.map/.filter/.slice`; EarningsCard uses `_.sumBy`; DeliveryAnalyticsCard uses `_.sortBy`; CampaignsAdsCard uses `_.chain/.sumBy` (note line 42 `_.toLowerCase()` is a pre-existing bug — it calls with no argument). For simple single-method files use e.g. `import sortBy from 'lodash/sortBy';` and replace `_.sortBy(` with `sortBy(`. For `_.chain(...)` files, chaining is not tree-shakeable per-method, so rewrite to plain composition (e.g. `orderBy(groupBy(data,'category'), ...)`) using named imports from `lodash`, or migrate the whole frontend to `lodash-es` (add `lodash-es` + `@types/lodash-es`, import `{ sortBy } from 'lodash-es'`) which tree-shakes natively under Vite.
After deletion, run a type check / build to surface any dangling references: `cd Frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` then `npm run build`. Fix any remaining barrel imports the compiler flags.

If, after removing all 6 files, no source under `Frontend/src` imports lodash anymore (re-run the grep to confirm), remove `"lodash": "^4.17.21"` from `dependencies` and `"@types/lodash": "^4.17.17"` from `devDependencies` in `Frontend/package.json`, then `npm install` to update the lockfile. Skip this step if any kept file still imports lodash.
verification: Run `grep -rn "import \* as _ from 'lodash'" Frontend/src` and confirm zero matches.
Run `cd Frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` — must complete with no errors (catches dangling barrel exports after deletion).
Run `cd Frontend && npm run build` — Vite build succeeds; the lodash chunk no longer appears (check the build output / `dist/assets` for any `lodash` chunk).

Smoke-test the running app (`npm run dev`) and click through the routed pages from `src/routes/routes.tsx` (Users, Timeline, Certificate, How-To-Use, reports) to confirm no missing-module runtime errors — these demo files were never routed, so behavior should be identical.
pitfalls: The audit lists only 3 locations but the repo actually has 6 files with this pattern (EarningsCard, CampaignsAdsCard, DeliveryAnalyticsCard were missed) — fix all 6 or the bundle pattern persists. Order matters: delete the barrel re-export lines in the same commit as deleting the files, otherwise `tsc`/Vite will fail to resolve the missing module. Do NOT remove lodash from package.json until grep confirms no remaining source imports it. lodash `chain()` (Faqs.tsx, Help.tsx, CampaignsAdsCard.tsx) is the one case that does not tree-shake via per-method imports — if those files are kept rather than deleted, either rewrite the chain into plain function composition or move to lodash-es. Note CampaignsAdsCard.tsx:42 `_.toLowerCase()` is called with no argument (a latent bug); deleting the file is the safest resolution rather than salvaging it. The verifier flagged that the 'tens of KB shipped to users' impact is likely overstated since these are unrouted (code-split out of the prod bundle), so this is low-priority cleanup, not a perf regression fix.

approach: Extract each tbody row into a React.memo'd Row component fed by stable useCallback'd renderCell/getRowKey, so unrelated re-renders skip the body.
steps: Open Frontend/src/components/My Components/Table/BasicTable.tsx. Note `useCallback` is NOT currently imported (line 1 imports only `useEffect, useMemo, useState`). Either add `useCallback` to that import or reference it as `React.useCallback` as shown in the patch.

Add a `BasicTableRow` component just above `export const BasicTable` (after `buildLegacyUrl`, ~line 119). Define `BasicTableRowInner<T extends AnyObject>` rendering the `<tr>` exactly as the current inline body row (lines 402-422): row-number `<td>`, the `normalizedColumns.map` cell `<td>`s calling `renderCell`, and the optional action `<td>` (`actionComponent` via `React.createElement` else `<TableAction id={row['id']} />`). Wrap it: `const BasicTableRow = React.memo(BasicTableRowInner) as typeof BasicTableRowInner;` — the cast preserves the generic signature React.memo would otherwise widen.

Pass the already-computed row number into the Row as a prop (`rowNumber={index + 1 + query.pageIndex * pageSize}`) instead of recomputing inside the Row, so the Row does not need `pageIndex`/`pageSize` and stays cheap to compare.

Replace the `getRowKey` function (lines 264-270) with a `React.useCallback` version, deps `[rowKey, pageIndex]` (it reads `pageIndex` in the fallback key). This keeps `getRowKey` referentially stable across loading/error re-renders.

Replace the `renderCell` function (lines 272-281) with a `React.useCallback` version, deps `[]` — it only closes over its arguments and the per-column `column.render`, so an empty dep array is correct and gives a permanently stable reference (required for `React.memo` on the Row to actually skip).

In the tbody (lines 401-423), replace the inline `<tr>...</tr>` returned by `data.data.map` with `<BasicTableRow<T> ... />`, forwarding `row`, `index`, `rowNumber`, `columns={normalizedColumns}`, `showRowNumber`, `showActions={shouldShowActions}`, `actionComponent`, and `renderCell`. Keep `key={getRowKey(row, index)}` on the `<BasicTableRow>` element.
Leave the skeleton `<tbody>` (434-454), the empty-state `<tr>` (425-429), the `<tfoot>` total row (456-488), and the `<thead>` untouched — only the data-row map changes.
Run the frontend type-check/lint (`npm run lint` / `tsc`) from Frontend/ to confirm the generic cast and new component compile with no `any` regressions.
verification: From Frontend/, run `npm run build` (or `npx tsc --noEmit`) — must compile clean; the `as typeof BasicTableRowInner` cast must preserve `<BasicTableRow<T> />` generic usage with no TS errors.
Run `npm run lint` from Frontend/ — no new warnings (especially react-hooks/exhaustive-deps on the new useCallback hooks).

Load any report page (e.g. via GenericReportPage), apply filters to populate the grid, set page size to 100 or 1000. Open React DevTools Profiler, enable 'Highlight updates when components render', then type in the company filter / trigger an unrelated parent state change — the body `BasicTableRow`s should NOT flash/re-render, only the changed parts should.

Confirm rendered output is byte-identical to before: row numbering (`index + 1 + pageIndex*pageSize`), cell values (`value?.toString() ?? 'N/A'`), custom `column.render` cells, action column, and the Excel export (`document.getElementById(tableId)` table_to_book) all still work.
Confirm pagination still updates row numbers correctly after changing page — the `rowNumber` prop must reflect the new `pageIndex`.
pitfalls: React.memo erases the generic type parameter; without `as typeof BasicTableRowInner` the call site `<BasicTableRow<T> />` will fail to type-check. Keep the cast.; renderCell's useCallback dep array must stay `[]`. If you accidentally add `normalizedColumns` or `data`, the reference changes every render and React.memo on the Row stops skipping — you get zero benefit (this is exactly the verifier's warning that useCallback alone is useless without the memoized Row, and vice-versa).; Do not move the row-number computation back inside the Row using `pageIndex`/`pageSize` — passing the precomputed `rowNumber` prop keeps the Row's prop set minimal and its shallow comparison fast.; Default shallow compare is correct here because all props (`row`, `columns`=`normalizedColumns` from useMemo, the useCallback'd `renderCell`, primitives) are referentially stable across unrelated re-renders. Do NOT add a custom `areEqual` comparator — it is unnecessary and risks stale cells if `row` identity changes on real data updates.; Severity is Low and the verifier confirmed real-world impact is a few ms even at the 1000-row max (cheap string cells, default pageSize 10). This is a perf hygiene change, not a correctness fix — keep it scoped to the body map and ship it alongside (not instead of) the separately-reported table-virtualization work. Rollback is trivial: revert the three edits, restore the inline `<tr>`.; If you choose to add `useCallback` to the line-1 import instead of using `React.useCallback`, update both new hooks consistently.

approach: Swap the wrapper nesting in `Frontend/src/main.tsx` so `<Provider store={store}>` is the outermost element and `<PersistGate persistor={persistor}>` sits directly inside it — the idiomatic redux-persist ordering that guarantees the store is in React context for any component PersistGate renders and that gating happens within the Redux tree. No store.ts changes are needed.
steps: Open Frontend/src/main.tsx. The current render tree (lines 12-20) nests <PersistGate persistor={persistor}> as the outer element wrapping <Provider store={store}>.

Invert the two wrappers: make <Provider store={store}> the outer element (just inside <React.StrictMode>) and move <PersistGate persistor={persistor}> to sit directly inside <Provider>, wrapping <AuthProvider>/<StepProvider>/<App />.

Keep the existing imports unchanged — `store` and `persistor` are both imported on line 5 from './redux/store.ts', and the Provider (react-redux) / PersistGate (redux-persist/integration/react) imports on lines 6-7 stay as-is.

Optionally (recommended for the 'default-theme flash' concern) pass a loading fallback to PersistGate, e.g. loading={null}, so children are not rendered until rehydration completes. This is the gating the swap actually enables.
Save and let Vite hot-reload; no changes to Frontend/src/redux/store.ts are required since persistStore/persistReducer are already configured correctly there (lines 23-35).
patch:
// Frontend/src/main.tsx  (render block)

// BEFORE
ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <PersistGate persistor={persistor}>
      <Provider store={store}>
        <AuthProvider>
          <StepProvider>
            <App />
          </StepProvider>
        </AuthProvider>
      </Provider>
    </PersistGate>
  </React.StrictMode>
);

// AFTER
ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <Provider store={store}>
      <PersistGate loading={null} persistor={persistor}>
        <AuthProvider>
          <StepProvider>
            <App />
          </StepProvider>
        </AuthProvider>
      </PersistGate>
    </Provider>
  </React.StrictMode>
);
verification: Run the frontend (npm run dev in Frontend/) and confirm the app loads with no console errors and the persisted theme (themeSlice) is applied without a visible default-theme flash on refresh.
In React DevTools, confirm Provider is now the parent of PersistGate (Provider outer, PersistGate inner).
Hard-refresh the page; the previously selected theme (stored under localStorage key 'persist:root') should be active immediately on first paint rather than flashing the default and then switching.
Build check: npm run build in Frontend/ succeeds with no TypeScript errors.
pitfalls: Per the verifier note, PersistGate reads the persistor from its own `persistor` prop (not Redux context), so the app was not actually broken before — this is correctness/ordering hardening, not a bug fix. Adding `loading={null}` slightly changes behavior: children render only after rehydration; if any top-level component relies on rendering before hydration, verify it still mounts. Rollback is trivial — revert the single render block in main.tsx. No data migration, no store.ts changes.

approach: Rewrite the breadcrumb logic in PageHeader.tsx so the LAST crumb renders the human-readable `title` prop (which GenericReportPage already passes as `config.title`) instead of the raw URL segment, never collapses to '...', and uses CSS ellipsis (full text kept in DOM + `title` attribute) for overflow. Map intermediate URL segments to friendly labels and delete the dead line-16 initializer.
steps: Open Frontend/src/components/PageHeader/PageHeader.tsx. The component already receives the readable name via its `title: string` prop (GenericReportPage.tsx line 736 calls `<PageHeader title={config.title} />`); the bug is that the breadcrumb ignores `title` and is built from `location.pathname.split('/')`.

Delete the dead initializer on line 16 (`const temp = [...'Home', location.pathname.toString().split('/')];`) and the `temp` reference in the `useState` on line 17. Replace the `useState`/`useEffect` pair with a single derivation of the path segments, e.g. `const segments = location.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean);` (drops the empty leading segment so the Home crumb is handled explicitly). You can remove the `useState`/`useEffect` imports if no longer used elsewhere in the file.

Add a small label map above the component for intermediate segments so machine names become readable, e.g. `const SEGMENT_LABELS: Record<string, string> = { Report: 'Reports', User: 'Users', dashboards: 'Dashboards' };` and a helper `const labelFor = (seg: string) => SEGMENT_LABELS[seg] ?? seg;`.

Rebuild the `item` array: index 0 stays the Home `<Link to="/"><HomeOutlined/></Link>` crumb. For the LAST crumb, render the human `title` prop (not the URL segment) inside a `<Typography.Text type="secondary">` that has the CSS ellipsis class and a native `title={title}` attribute so the full name stays in the DOM and on hover.
